Cutar trachoma ta kasance wata matsala a wasu bangare mafi talauci na duniya, inda mutane sukan zauna a kananan kauyuka da kuma yankuna masu nisa, kuma suna da matakin samun ruwa, tsafta, da kula da lafiya mai karanci sosai.  Afrika ce ta fi jinya da wannan cutar, amma kuma ta na da kokarin kawar da cutar a mafi yawan wurare. Cutar ta kuma bayyana a Kudancin Amurka da Amurka ta Tsakiya, Asiya, Ostiraliya, da Gabas ta Tsakiya.  A ranar 2 ga Janairu, 2020, kasashe 13 sun bayyana an samu nasara wajen kawar da cutar.

translations.source: In 2019 alone, 92 622 people received surgical treatment for advanced trachoma and 95.2 million were treated with antibiotics. Despite these successes, the disease remains a serious public health concern, with an estimated US$ 8 billion annual loss in productivity due to blindness and visual impairment. Symptoms: Infection is associated with inflammatory changes in the conjunctiva known as “active trachoma”. An individual’s immune system can clear a single episode of infection, but in endemic communities, re-acquisition of the organism occurs frequently. After years of repeated episodes of active trachoma, the inside of the eyelid can become so severely scarred (trachomatous conjunctival scarring) that it turns inwards and causes the eyelashes to rub against the eyeball (trachomatous trichiasis), resulting in constant pain and light intolerance; this and other alterations of the eye can lead to scarring of the cornea.
translations.reference: A shekarar 2019 kaɗai, an yi wa mutane 92 622 aikin tiyata don babbar cutar ta trachoma, kuma an yi wa mutum miliyan 95.2 maganin da magungunan kashe ƙwayoyin cuta. Duk da waɗannan nasarorin, cutar ta ci gaba da zama babbar damuwa ga lafiyar jama'a, tare da asarar kusan dalar Amurka biliyan 8 a kowace shekara wurin yin aiki saboda makanta da matsalar gani. Alamomi: Kamuwa da cutar na haɗe da canje-canje na kumburi a cikin conjunctiva da ake kira "trachoma mai aiki". Garkuwar jikin mutum na iya kawar da dunƙulen cutar guda ɗaya, amma a cikin al'ummomin da cutar tayi yawa, sake haɗuwa da ƙwayoyin cutar yana faruwa akai-akai. Bayan shekaru da yawa ana maimaita cutar ta trachoma mai aiki, cikin fatar ido zai iya zama mai tabo mai tsanani (trachomatous conjunctival scarring) har ta juya zuwa ciki tana saka gashin ido yana gogar ƙwayar ido (trachomatous trichiasis), wanda ke haifar da ciwo na kullum da rashin iya kallon haske sosai; wannan da kuma sauran canje-canjen ido na iya haifar da tabon cornea.

Duk da wannan nasara, cutar har yanzu ta kasance barazana mai gaske ga lafiya ta jama'a, a kiyashi asarar kudin da ake samu a kowace shekara na dala Amurka biliyan 8 saboda lalacewar ido da raguwar gani. Alamomin: Cutar ta haɗu da canje-canje na ciwo a cikin kwanfitan ido, wanda ake kira "active trachoma." Tsarin jinin mutum na iya kawar da wata matsala ta cuta, amma a cikin al'ummomin da cutar ta yi yawa, sake samun cutar ya faru akai-akai. Bayan shekaru na jarrabawar cutar trachoma, ciki na kwakwalwa na iya zama mai tsanani (trachomatous conjunctival scarring) har ya juya cikin gaba kuma ya sa eyelashes su sha shafi kwanan ido (trachomatous trichiasis), wanda ke haifar da ciwo da kuma jimaɓawar haske; wannan da sauran canje-canje a idon na iya haifar da lalacewar cornea.

**Notes on Translation Choices & Considerations:**

*   **Technical Terms:** I've tried to use the most appropriate Hausa terms for medical concepts.  "Active trachoma" and "trachomatous conjunctival scarring" and "trachomatous trichiasis" are kept as close to the English as possible, as direct, widely understood Hausa equivalents are lacking.  It's important to note that in a real-world scenario, consultation with a Hausa-speaking medical professional would be ideal to ensure the most accurate and culturally appropriate terminology.
*   **Numbers:** Numbers are written as they are commonly used in Hausa.
*   **Clarity and Flow:** I've aimed for a translation that is not only accurate but also reads naturally in Hausa.
*   **"Despite these successes":** Translated as "Duk da wannan nasara" - "Despite this success."
*   **"Re-acquisition of the organism":** Translated as "sake samun cutar" - "re-obtaining the disease." This is a more natural phrasing in Hausa than a literal translation.

As an AI, my translation is based on my training data and algorithms. This is especially important for medical information.

translations.source: Left untreated, this condition leads to the formation of irreversible opacities, with resulting visual impairment or blindness. The age at which this occurs depends on several factors including local transmission intensity. In very highly endemic communities, it can occur in childhood, though onset of visual impairment between the ages of 30 and 40 years is more typical. Women are blinded up to four times as often as men, probably due to more frequent contact with infected children. Individuals living in the poorest and most remote communities of Africa, Asia, Australia, the Middle East, Central and South America and the Pacific Islands are at risk.
translations.reference: Idan ba a yi maganin sa ba, yana iya haifar da haɗuwar dusashewar da ba za a iya gyarawa ba, tare da haifar da naƙasar gani ko makanta. Shekarun da wannan ke faruwa ya dogara da abubuwa da yawa, waɗanda suka haɗa da ƙarfin yaɗuwa na cikin gida. A cikin al'ummomin da masu cutar ke da yawa sosai, tana iya faruwa a lokacin yarinta, ko da yake farkon nakasar gani ya fi faruwa tsakanin shekaru 30 zuwa 40. Mata sun fi makanta sau huɗu fiye da maza, mai yiwuwa saboda yawan saduwa da yara masu ɗauke da cutar. Mutanen da ke zaune a cikin mafi talauci kuma mafi nisan al'ummomin Afirka da Asiya da Ostiraliya da Gabas ta Tsakiya da Amurka ta Tsakiya da Amurka ta Kudu da Tsibiran Pacific suna cikin haɗari.

translations.source: Treatment: Trachoma can be prevented by limiting environmental risk factors. These include inadequate hygiene, crowded households, inadequate access to water and inadequate access to and use of sanitation. Infections usually begin at an early age and grow less frequent and shorter in duration with increasing age. Infections are most often acquired through family and contact with others with active disease. Repeated infections cause the most serious symptoms of the disease.
translations.reference: Magancewa: Ana iya kare cutar ta trachoma ta hanyar rage haɗurran da ke da alaƙa da muhalli. Waɗannan sun haɗa da rashin isasshiyar tsafta, da cunkoso a gidaje, da rashin samun isasshen ruwa, da rashin isassu kayan tsaftar muhalli da damar amfani da su. Yawanci cutar na farawa a ƙananan shekaru, kuma tana ƙaranci kuma mafi gaharta idan ana ƙara yawan shekaru. An fi kamuwa da cutar ta hanyar iyali da hulɗa da masu fama da cutar. Maimaita cutar na haifar da mafi girman alamun cutar.
translations.hypothesis:

**Hausa Translation:**

Tsari/Magani: Za'a iya hana cutar trachoma ta hanyar kawar da abubuwan da ke haifar da cuta a muhittacin mutane. Wadannan abubuwan sun haɗa da wahala a tsanwa, zama a cikin gida mai yawa, wahala a samu ruwa, da kuma wahala a samu da amfani da kayan aikin tsaftar muhalli. Kwarewar cutar yawanci ke farawa a shekarun matashi, kuma tana zubar da ƙarfi da tsawaita kusan kusan a kowace shekara da ta wuce. Kwarewar cutar yawanci ana samu ta hanyar iyali da kuma tuntuɓar mutanen da ke da cutar. Sabuntawar cutar ta kai ga alamomin da suka fi tsanani na cutar.

**Pronunciation Guide (approximate - Hausa pronunciation can vary by region):**

*   **Tsari/Magani:**  Tsa-ree / Ma-ga-nee

**Explanation of Choices and Potential Refinements:**

*   **"Treatment" - "Tsari/Magani":** I've provided both "Tsari" (meaning "method" or "procedure") and "Magani" (meaning "medicine" or "treatment") as options. *   **"Sanitation" - "kayan aikin tsaftar muhalli":** This translates to "tools/equipment for environmental cleanliness." It's a functional description.

translations.source: Trachoma can be eliminated as a public health problem using the SAFE strategy, comprising: Surgery to treat trachomatous trichiasis, the blinding stage of the disease; Antibiotics to clear infection, particularly mass drug administration of the antibiotic azithromycin, which is donated by the manufacturer to national programmes through the International Trachoma Initiative; Facial cleanliness; and Environmental improvement, particularly better access to water and sanitation.
translations.reference: Ana iya daƙile cutar ta trachoma a matsayin matsalar lafiyar jama'a ta hanyar amfani da dabarun SAFE, wanda ya ƙunshi: Tiyata don magance trachomatous trichiasis, matakin makanta na cutar; Magungunan kashe ƙwayoyin cuta don kawar da cutar, musamman bayar da maganin nan mai suna azithromycin mai kashe ƙwayoyin cuta da yawa, wanda masana'anta ke bayarwa kyauta ga shirye-shiryen ƙasa ta hanyar International Trachoma Initiative; Tsaftar fuska; da kuma Inganta muhalli, musamman samun damar amfani da ruwa da tsafta mafiya kyawu.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy suitable for a public health context. I've included some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Hausa Translation:**

Cutarsa na iya kawar da cutar trachoma a matsayin matsala ta lafiya ta jama'a ta amfani da tsarin SAFE, wanda ya ƙunshi:

*   **Cirewar ajiya (Surgery):** Don magance Trichiasis trachomatous, wacce ke haifar da lalacewar ido.
*   **Antibiyoyin (Antibiotics):** Don kawar da kamuwa da cuta, musamman rarraba kwayoyin cuta a baje ajiye, galibi ana amfani da azithromycin, wanda masana'anta ke ba da kyauta ga kungiyoyin kasa ta hanyar Kungiyar Kwararren Aikin Trachoma (International Trachoma Initiative).
*   **Tsarkakawar fuskoki (Facial cleanliness):**
*   **Ƙirƙirar yanayi mai kyau (Environmental improvement):** Musamman samun isassun ruwan kwalabure da tsafta.

**Pronunciation Guide (approximate - Hausa pronunciation can vary by region):**

*   **Cutarsa:**  Coo-tahr-sah
*   **Trachoma:**  Trah-koh-mah
*   **Matsala:**  Mah-tah-sah-lah

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **SAFE Strategy:** I kept "SAFE" as is, as it's a widely recognized acronym in the trachoma field.  It's common to use English acronyms in Hausa when discussing international health programs.

translations.source: WHO adopted the SAFE strategy in 1993 and continues in its mandate to provide leadership and coordination of international efforts to eliminate trachoma as a public health problem.
translations.reference: WHO ta yi amfani da dabarun SAFE a shekarar 1993, kuma tana ci gaba da aikinta na yin jagoranci da daidaita ayyukan ƙasa-da-ƙasa don kawar da cutar ta trachoma a matsayin matsalar lafiyar jama'a.

**Explanation of Choices:**

*   **WHO:** Remains WHO as it's a widely recognized abbreviation.

translations.source: Traditional, Complementary and Integrative Medicine Overview: Traditional medicine Traditional medicine has a long history. It is the sum total of the knowledge, skill, and practices based on the theories, beliefs, and experiences indigenous to different cultures, whether explicable or not, used in the maintenance of health as well as in the prevention, diagnosis, improvement or treatment of physical and mental illness. Complementary medicine
translations.reference: Magungunan Gargajiya da na Ƙari da Haɗaɗɗun Magunguna Bayani: Maganin gargajiya Maganin gargajiya yana da dogon tarihi. Shi ne jimillar ilimi da ƙwarewa da ayyuka bisa ƙa'idoji da imani da gogewa na cikin gida ga mutane masu al’adu daban-daban, wanda za a iya bayaninsa da wanda ba za a iya ba, wanda ake amfani da su wajen kula da lafiya da kuma rigakafi da gano cuta da ingantawa ko magance rashin lafiyar jiki da ta ƙwaƙwalwa. Maganin Ƙari
translations.hypothesis:

**Hausa Translation:**

**Bincike Akan Magani Na Al'ada, Magani Na Raka, da Magani Na Haɗawa:**

**Magani Na Al'ada:** Maganin Al'ada ya yi tarihi mai tsayi.  Shi ne haddi kuma na ilmi, ƙwarewa, da ayyukan da suka fi kowa bisa ga ra'ayoyi, imani, da ƙwarewar da suka mallaki al'ummomi daban-daban, ko ma da a bayyana ko aɓɓakawa, an yi amfani da su don kiyaye lafiya, da kuma hana, gano, inganta, ko magance cutar jiki da cutar rai.

**Magani Na Raka:** (This section is incomplete in the original English, so I'll provide a placeholder.  A full translation would require the rest of the English text.)

---

**Notes on Translation Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Traditional, Complementary and Integrative Medicine Overview"**: I translated this as "Bincike Akan Magani Na Al'ada, Magani Na Raka, da Magani Na Haɗawa."  "Bincike Akan" means "Overview/Study of."  "Magani" means "Medicine."  The rest are direct translations of the terms.
*   **"Traditional medicine"**:  "Maganin Al'ada" is the standard Hausa term for traditional medicine.
*   **"has a long history"**: "ya yi tarihi mai tsayi" - literally "has a long history."
*   **"sum total"**: "haddi kuma" - This conveys the idea of a comprehensive collection or totality.
*   **"knowledge, skill, and practices"**: "ilmi, ƙwarewa, da ayyuka" - These are standard Hausa words for these concepts.
*   **"theories, beliefs, and experiences"**: "ra'ayoyi, imani, da ƙwarewar" - Direct translations.
*   **"indigenous to different cultures"**: "da suka mallaki al'ummomi daban-daban" - "Mallaki" means "to possess/own" in this context, indicating belonging to different cultures.

translations.source: The terms “complementary medicine” or “alternative medicine” refer to a broad set of health care practices that are not part of that country’s own tradition or conventional medicine and are not fully integrated into the dominant health-care system. They are used interchangeably with traditional medicine in some countries. Herbal medicines Herbal medicines include herbs, herbal materials, herbal preparations and finished herbal products, that contain as active ingredients parts of plants, or other plant materials, or combinations. Impact: The WHO Traditional Medicine Strategy 2014–2023 was developed and launched in response to the World Health Assembly resolution on traditional medicine (WHA62.13).
translations.reference: "Maganin ƙari " ko "maganin madadi " na nufin wani jerin tsarin ayyukan kula da lafiya waɗanda ba sa cikin al'adar ƙasar na kanta ko magungunan gargajiya kuma ba sa cikin gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya. Ana amfani da su a madadin magungunan gargajiya a wasu ƙasashe. Magungunan ganye Magungunan ganye sun haɗa da ganyayyaki da kayan ganyayyaki da gyara ganyayyayki da kuma kayan ganye da aka sarrafa, waɗanda ke ƙunshe da sassan tsirrai ko wasu kayan shuka ko duka. Tasiri: An ƙirƙira kuma an ƙaddamar da Dabarun Magungunan Gargajiya na WHO 2014-2023 don mayar da martani ga ƙudurin Majalisar Lafiya ta Duniya kan magungunan gargajiya (WHA62.13).

translations.source: The strategy aims to support Member States in developing proactive policies and implementing action plans that will strengthen the role traditional medicine plays in keeping populations healthy. Addressing the challenges, responding to the needs identified by Member States and building on the work done under the WHO traditional medicine strategy: 2002–2005, the updated strategy for the period 2014–2023 devotes more attention than its predecessor to prioritizing health services and systems, including traditional and complementary medicine products, practices and practitioners. The strategic objectives are: To build the knowledge base for active management of T&CM through appropriate national policies; To strengthen the quality assurance, safety, proper use and effectiveness of T&CM by regulating products, practices and practitioners;
translations.reference: Dabarun na da nufin tallafawa ƙasashe mambobin ƙungiyar wuein samar da tsare-tsare na gaske da aiwatar da tsare-tsaren ayyuka waɗanda za su ƙarfafa rawar da magungunan gargajiya ke takawa wajen kiyaye lafiyar al'umma. Magance ƙalubalen, mayar da martani ga buƙatun da ƙasashe mambobi suka gano, da kuma haɓaka aikin da aka yi a ƙarƙashin dabarun maganin gargajiya na WHO: 2002-2005, dabarun da aka sabunta a lokacin 2014-2023 ya ba da ƙaimi fiye da wanda ya gabace shi don ba da fifiko ga ayyuka da tsare-tsaren kiwon lafiya, waɗanda suka haɗa magungunan gargajiya da na ƙari, da ayyuka masu aiki. Manufofin dabarun su ne: Don gina fage na ilimi don gudanar da aikin T&CM ta hanyar tsare-tsare na ƙasa waɗanda suka dace; Don ƙarfafa tabbatar da inganci da aminci da amfani da T & CM yadda ya dace kuma cikin tasiri ta hanyar kula da magunguna da ayyuka da masu aiki;

translations.source: To promote universal health coverage by integrating T&CM services into health care service delivery and self-health care. WHO response: The WHO global report on traditional and complementary medicine 2019 was developed to address the gap in reliable, credible and official data from Member States in the area of T&CM. This report reviews global progress in T&CM over the past two decades and is based on contributions from 179 WHO Member States. It provides valuable information for policy-makers, health professionals and the public for capitalizing on the potential contribution of T&CM to health and well-being.
translations.reference: Don haɓaka shiga ko ina a duniya ta hanyar haɗe ayyukan T&CM cikin gudanar da ayyukan kiwon lafiya da kula da lafiyar kai. Martanin WHO: an samar da rahoton na WHO na duniya game da maganin gargajiya da na ƙari na 2019 don cike giɓin bayanai abin dogaro, sahihi, kuma na hukuma daga Ƙasashe Mambobi na yankin T&CM. Wannan rahoto ya yi duba kan ci gaban duniya a T&CM cikin shekaru ashirin da suka gabata, kuma ya dogara ne kan gudummawar ƙasashe mambobin WHO guda 179. Ya ba da bayanai masu mahimmanci ga masu tsara manufofi, da ƙwararru kan kiwon lafiya, da jama'a don yin amfani da yiwuwar gudummawar T&CM ga lafiya da walwala.

translations.source: Transplantation Overview: Transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs Transplantation of human cells, tissues or organs saves many lives and restores essential functions where no alternatives of comparable effectiveness exist. In 50 years, transplantation has become a successful worldwide practice. However, there are large differences between countries in access to suitable transplantation and in the level of safety, quality, efficacy of donation and transplantation of human cells, tissues and organs.
translations.reference: Dashe Bayani: Dasa tantanin halitta da tishu da gaɓoɓin mutane Dashentantanin halitta ko tishu ko gaɓoɓin jikin mutum na ceton rayuka da yawa kuma yana dawo da ayyuka masu mahimmanci inda babu tasiri madadin su. Cikin shekaru 50, dashe yayi nasara a faɗin duniya. Sai dai akwai babban bambanci tsakanin ƙasashe wajen samun damar dashen da ya dace da kuma matakin aminci da inganci da samun gudummawa da dashen tantanin halitta na ɗan adam da tishu da gaɓoɓi.

translations.source: The ethical aspects of transplantation are at the forefront. In particular, the unmet patients’ needs and the shortage of transplants lead to the temptation of trafficking in human body components for transplantation. Human transplantation Human cells and tissues for transplantation can save lives or restore essential functions. For examplea corneal graft can restore sight in corneal blindness;
translations.reference: Abubuwan da suka dace na dashe su ne kan gaba. Musamman, rashin biyan buƙatun marasa lafiya da ƙarancin abin dashe na haifar da yiwuwar fataucin sassan jikin ɗan adam don dasawa. Dashen mutum Tantanin halitta na ɗan adam da tishu don dasawa na iya ceton rayuka ko dawo da ayyuka masu mahimmanci. Misali corneal graft zai iya dawo da gani a makantar corneal;

translations.source: the transplantation of haematopoietic stem cells can cure congenital or acquired diseases including some leukaemias See further information on Haematopoeitic Stem Cells the transplantation of a human heart valve often constitutes the best replacement situation and recipient patients do not require long term anti-coagulation therapy. Xenotransplantation Xenotransplantation, animal to human, defined as living cells, tissues or organs of animal origin and human body fluids, cells, tissues or organs that have ex vivo with these living, xenogeneic materials, has the potential to constitute an alternative to material of human origin and bridge the shortfall in human material for transplantation.
translations.reference: dashen tantanin halitta na hematopoietic na iya warkar da cututtukan da aka haifi mutum da su ko waɗanda suka samu, waɗanda suka haɗa da wasu daga cikin cutar sankarar bargo. Duba ƙarin bayani akan Tantanin Halitta na Haematopoeitic Sau da yawa dashen bawul na zuciyar ɗan adam yakan zama mafi kyawun yanayin maye gurbi kuma marasa lafiya masu karɓa ba sa buƙatar dogon lokaci na maganin cututtukan sarƙewar jini. Xenotransplantation Xenotransplantation, wato daga dabba zuwa mutum, shi ne rayayyen tantanin halitta ko tishu ko gaɓoɓin asali na dabba da ruwan jikin mutum da tantanin halitta ko tishu ko gaɓoɓin da ke da ex vivo da waɗannan masu ran, da kayan xenogeneic, suna da yiwuwar zama madadin kayan asali na jikin ɗan adam da kuma cike giɓin da ke cikin kayan jikin ɗan adam don dasawa.

translations.source: Donation: Donations from deceased persons should be developed to their maximum therapeutic potential avoiding, whenever possible, the inherent risks to live donors, as stated in WHO Guiding Principle 3. There is a recognized need for communities, and health professionals, to become better educated about donation and transplantation and that is the key to the success of deceased donation programmes. However, despite the frequent use of materials donated from deceased donors, the donations from living donors are necessary for some types of transplants or to compensate for the limited supply of material available from deceased donors in order to meet patient needs. Living donation is thus practised despite the fact that it involves risks for the donor that may not be negligible. As the procurement of human material for transplantation from deceased or living donors and the subsequent allogeneic transplantation may entail ethical and safety risks for both the recipient and the donor, strict controls and effective oversight should be carried out by the health authorities to protect them.
translations.reference: Gudunmawa: Ya kamata a haɓaka gudummawa daga matattu zuwa iyakar ƙarfinsu don gujewa haɗarin da ke tattare da masu ba da gudummawa gwargwadon iko, kamar yadda aka bayyana a cikin Ƙa’idodin Jagora na 3. Akwai buƙata ga al'ummomi da ƙwararru kan kiwon lafiya ta samun ilimi mai zurfi game da gudummawa da dashe, kuma wannan shi ne mabuɗin samun nasarar shirye-shiryen bayar da gudummawa daga waɗanda da suka mutu. Duk da haka, duk da yawan amfani da kayan da aka ba da gudummawa daga matattu, gudummawar da ake bayarwa daga masu ba da gudummawar rayuwa ya zama dole don wasu nau'ikan dashe ko don rama ƙarancin kayan da ake samu daga masu ba da gudummawar da suka mutu don biyan buƙatun majiyyata. Don haka, ana ba da gudummawar rayuwa duk da cewa tana ƙunshe da haɗari ga mai ba da gudummawa wanda ba za a yi sakaci da shi ba. Yayin da sayan kayan ɗan adam na dashe daga masu bayarwa ga matattu ko masu rai, kuma dashen allogeneic na gaba na iya haifar da haɗari na ɗabi'a da aminci ga duka masu karɓa da mai ba da gudummawa da tsauraran kula da ingantaccen sa ido ya kamata hukumomin lafiya su aiwatar da su don kare su.

translations.source: The Guiding Principles mandate optimal care for donors and recipients. The transparent oversight of the health authorities over donation and transplantation activities is also essential to increase the trust of the public in the system. In addition, the decision to be a donor is often based on the understanding that a contribution to the availability of transplant resources may someday benefit the health needs of the donor’s family. If we are prepared to receive a transplant should we need one, then we should be ready to give Ethics: WHO Guiding Principles on Human Cell, Tissue and Organ Transplantation
translations.reference: Ƙa'idodin Jagora sun ba da umarni mai kyakkyawar kulawa ga masu bayarwa da masu karɓa. Sa ido a fili na hukumomin lafiya kan ayyukan bayar da agaji da ayyukan dashe yana da matuƙar muhimmanci wajen ƙara amincewar jama'a kan tsarin. Bugu da ƙari, sau da dama yanke shawarar zama mai bayarwa ya dogara ne kan fahimtar cewa gudummawar da aka samu don samar da dashen zai iya amfanar da buƙatun lafiyar iyalin mai bayarwa. Idan mun shirya karɓar dasshe idan muna da buƙatar hakan, to ya kamata mu kasance a shirye don bayarwa Ɗa'a: Ƙa'idodin Jagora na WHO kan Dashen Tantanin halitta da Tishu da Gaɓoɓin Jiki

translations.source: In 1987 the fortieth World Health Assembly, concerned at the trade for profit in human organs, initiated the preparation of the first WHO Guiding Principles on Transplantation, endorsed by the Assembly in 1991 in resolution WHA44.25. These Guiding Principles have greatly influenced professional codes and practices as well as legislation around the world during almost two decades. After a consultation process that took several years, the Sixty-third World Health Assembly adopted resolution WHA63.22 on 21 May 2010, endorsing the updated WHO Guiding Principles and identifying areas of progress to optimize donation and transplantation practices. The Declaration of Istanbul Professionals of donation and transplantation from all regions, through many of their organizations and institutions, are endorsing the Declaration of Istanbul on organ trafficking and transplant tourism developed under the leadership of the Transplantation Society and the International Society of Nephrology in May 2008.
translations.reference: A 1987, Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya ta arba'in, ta damu da kasuwanci don riba kan sassan jikin mutum, ta fara shirye-shiryen Ƙa'idodin Jagora na farko na WHO game da dashe, wanda Majalisar ta amince da shi a 1991 a ƙuduri na WHA44.25. Waɗannan Ƙa'idodin Jagora sun yi tasiri sosai ga ƙa'idodin ƙwararru da ayyuka da kuma dokoki a duk duniya cikin kusan shekaru ashirin. Bayan tuntuɓa wadda ta ɗauki shekaru da yawa, Majalisar Kiwon Lafiya ta Duniya ta Sittin da Uku ta amince da ƙuduri na WHA63.22 a ranar 21 ga Mayu 2010, tare da amincewa da sabunta Ƙa'idodin Jagororanci na WHO da gano wuraren ci gaba don inganta ayyukan ba da gudummawa da dasawa. Sanarwar Istanbul Ƙwararru na ba da gudummawa da dasawa daga dukan yankuna, ta hanyar ƙungiyoyi da cibiyoyi da yawa, suna goyon bayan Sanarwar Istanbul game da fataucin sassan jiki da yawon shaƙatawa da aka bunƙasa a ƙarƙashin jagorancin ƙungiyar dashe da kuma Ƙungiya Nephrology ta Ƙasa-da-ƙasa a watan Mayun 2008.

Waɗannan Dokokin Jagoranci sun ƙara ƙarfafa ƙudura da aikin gaba da dokokin duniya a cikin kusan shekaranni biyu. Bayan taron amintacce da ya ɗauki shekaru tsawon shekaru, Majalisar Kula da Lafiya ta Duniya ta uku da ta uku ta amince da ƙudirin WHA63.22 a ranar 21 ga Mayu, 2010, wanda ya amince da Dokokin Jagorancin WHO da suka sabunta, da kuma gano fannoni na ci gaba don haɓaka ayyukan ba da abubuwan ciki da kuma transplant. Sanarwar Istanbul ta ƙwararrun aikin ba da abubuwan ciki da transplant daga dukkan yankuna, ta hanyar kungiyoyi da jiga-jigansu da yawa, suna amincewa da Sanarwar Istanbul kan cinikin ƙungiyoyin jiki da kuma yawon transplant da aka ƙirƙira a ƙarƙashin jagorancin Kungiyar Transplant da kuma Kungiyar Kiba ta Kasa a watan Mayu, 2008.

translations.source: Travel and health Overview: International travel can pose various risks to health, depending on the characteristics of both the traveller and the travel. Travellers may encounter sudden and significant changes in altitude, humidity, microbes, and temperature, which can result in ill-health. In addition, serious health risks may arise in areas where accommodation is of poor quality, hygiene and sanitation are inadequate, medical services are not well developed and clean water is unavailable. International travel is on the rise, as millions of people travel for professional, social, recreational and humanitarian purposes each year.
translations.reference: Tafiya da lafiya Taƙaitaccen Bayani: Tafiya tsakanin ƙasashe na iya barazana na haɗari daban-daban ga lafiya, wanda ya danganta da yanayin matafiyin da ita tafiyar. Matafiya na iya haɗuwa da mahimman canje-canje a lokaci guda a cikin ladabi da yanayin zafi da ƙwayoyin halitta da zafin jiki waɗanda zasu iya haifar da rashin lafiya. Bugu da ƙari, munanan haɗarurruka ga lafiya na iya fitowa a cikin wuraren da babu wadata da ingancin tsaftar jiki da muhalli, ayyukan likitanci ba a tsara su da kyau ba haka kuma babu tsaftataccen ruwa. Tafiya tsakanin ƙasashe na ƙaruwa, yadda ya zama miliyoyin mutane ke tafiya don aiki, zamantakewa, nishaɗi da kuma dalilai na aikin jin ƙaidalilai kowace shekara.

translations.source: All travellers must prepare for the variety of health risks they can be exposed to in unfamiliar environments before, during and after they travel. People planning to travel should seek advice on the potential hazards in their chosen destinations and understand how best to protect their health and minimize the risk of acquiring disease. Forward planning, appropriate preventive measures and careful precautions can protect the health of travellers, and minimize the risks of accident and of acquiring disease. Although the medical profession and the travel industry can provide extensive help and sound advice, it remains the traveller’s responsibility to seek information, to understand the risks involved and to take the necessary precautions to protect their health while travelling. Risk: When determining the health risks of international travel, travellers and health professionals should consider the following key factors:
translations.reference: Dole ne dukkanin matafiya su shirya don nau'ikan haɗari ga lafiya da za a iya samu cikin yanayin da ba a sani ba a kafin da yayin da kuma bayan tafiya. Ya kamata mutane da ke shirin tafiya su nemi shawara kan haɗarin da za su iya samu a wurin da zasu je kuma su fahimci hanyar da zasu kare lafiyarsu da kuma yadda zasu rage haɗarin kamuwa da cuta. Shirin gaba sune, matakan da suka dace da taka tsantsan na iya kare lafiyar matafiya, kuma suna rage haɗarin haɗari da samun cuta. Kodayake masana'antar likitanci da masana'antar tafiye-tafiye na iya samar da taimako mai yawa da kuma shawara kyau, ya kasance da alhakin matafiya ne neman bayanai da fahimtar haɗarurrukan da ke ƙunshe da kuma ɗaukan matakan kariya da suka dace don kiyaye lafiyarsu yayin tafiya. translations.source: mode of transport destination(s) duration and season of travel purpose of travel standards of accommodation, food hygiene and sanitation

translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided English phrases, aiming for clarity and natural-sounding language. I'll also include some notes on choices made to ensure accuracy and cultural relevance.

**English:** mode of transport destination(s) duration and season of travel purpose of travel standards of accommodation, food hygiene and sanitation

**Hausa:**

*   **Mode of transport:** *Hanyar tafiya* (Literally: "way of traveling") or *Abun da ake amfani da shi don tafiya* (More descriptive: "The thing used for traveling") - The first option is more concise and common.
*   **Destination(s):** *Makomocin* (Plural of *Makomi* - destination) or *Inda za su je* (Where they will go) - *Makomocin* is more formal and suitable for a document.
*   **Duration and season of travel:** *Lokaci da kwanaki na tafiya* (Time and days of travel) or *Tsawon lokacin tafiya da lokacin shekara* (Length of travel time and season of the year) - The first is more concise.
*   **Purpose of travel:** *Dalilin tafiya* (Reason for travel)
*   **Standards of accommodation:** *Matsalolin wurin zama* (Standards of lodging/place to stay) or *Ingancin wurin zama* (Quality of lodging/place to stay) - *Matsalolin* is more formal.
*   **Food hygiene:** *Tsarkin abinci* (Cleanliness of food)
*   **Sanitation:** *Tsarkin muhalli* (Environmental cleanliness) or *Tsafta* (Cleanliness - more general) - *Tsarkin muhalli* is more specific to sanitation.

**Combined Translation (for the entire phrase):**

**Hausa:** *Hanyar tafiya, makomocin, lokaci da kwanaki na tafiya, dalilin tafiya, matsalolin wurin zama, tsarkin abinci, da tsarkin muhalli.*

**Explanation of Choices and Considerations:**

*   **Formal vs. Informal:** I've leaned towards more formal language (*Matsalolin* instead of *Ingancin*) as the original text seems to be for a document or form.
*   **Clarity:**  Hausa sometimes requires more descriptive phrasing than English to convey the same meaning.
*   **Cultural Context:**  The concept of "sanitation" can be broad. *Tsarkin muhalli* (environmental cleanliness) is often the most relevant interpretation in this context.
*   **Word Choice:** I've provided alternatives where appropriate, allowing for slight variations in tone and emphasis.



I hope this translation is helpful!  If you have more text to translate, please provide it, and I'll do my best to assist you.

**Transliteration (for easier reading/pronunciation):**

Canjin yanayi, irin su tsayin bango, zafin kumshi, da tsabta, cututtukan abinci da ruwa, rashin isassiyar hidimar lafiya, raunanan jiki, gami da hatsarin mota ko wasanni, lafiyar ilimin hankali, gami da damuwa da tsokice mai al'ada.

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **"Environmental changes" - "Canjin yanayi":** This is a standard and widely understood translation.
*   **"Altitude" - "Tsayin bango":**  "Bango" means "wall" or "height," and "tsayin" means "height of." This is a common way to express altitude in Hausa.
*   **"Humidity" - "Zafin kumshi":** "Zafi" means "heat" and "kumshi" means "moisture/humidity."  This is a common and understandable way to convey the concept.
*   **"Temperature" - "Tsabta":** This is a tricky one.  While there are more technical terms for temperature, "tsabta" (meaning "cleanliness" or "purity") is often used colloquially to refer to the degree of heat or cold.  It's a compromise for broader understanding.  A more formal translation might use a borrowed word, but that can reduce accessibility.
*   **"Foodborne and waterborne diseases" - "Cututtukan abinci da ruwa":**  Straightforward translation. "Cututtuka" means "diseases," "abinci" means "food," and "ruwa" means "water."
*   **"Inadequate medical services" - "Rashin isassiyar hidimar lafiya":** "Rashin" means "lack of," "isassiya" means "sufficiency," and "hidimar lafiya" means "medical care/services."
*   **"Injuries" - "Raunanan jiki":** "Raunana" means "injury" and "jiki" means "body."
*   **"Including" - "Gami da":** This is a standard way to say "including" or "such as."
*   **"Road traffic accidents" - "Hatsarin mota":** "Hatsari" means "accident" and "mota" means "vehicle/car."
*   **"Recreational activities" - "Wasanni":** "Wasanni" generally means "sports" or "games," but it's often used to encompass recreational activities.
*   **"Psychological health" - "Lafiyar ilimin hankali":** "Lafiya" means "health," "ilimi" means "knowledge/science," and "hankali" means "mind/intellect."
*   **"Stress" - "Damuwa":** This is the standard Hausa word for "stress."
*   **"Culture shock" - "Tsokice mai al'ada":** "Tsokice" means "surprise/shock" and "mai al'ada" means "traditional/customary." This attempts to capture the feeling of disorientation from a new culture.

**Important Notes for Low-Resource Language Translation:**

*   **Context is Key:**  Without more context, some terms are translated using the most common or understandable equivalent, even if it's not a perfect technical match.
*   **Borrowing vs. Adaptation:** I've generally avoided borrowing English words directly, as this can make the translation inaccessible to those with limited English proficiency. Instead, I've tried to adapt existing Hausa words or phrases.

translations.source: Travellers may also be exposed to a number of infectious diseases, and many of these can be prevented by vaccination. However, there are some infectious diseases, including some of the most dangerous, for which no vaccines exist. Destinations where accommodation, hygiene and sanitation, medical care and water quality are of a high standard pose relatively few serious risks to the health of travellers. Where these services are not provided, travellers should take stringent precautions to avoid illness. Travel warnings from governmental sources should be taken seriously.
translations.reference: Hakanan matafiya zasu iya haɗuwa da cututtuka da dama, kuma da yawa daga cikin waɗannan za a iya daƙile su ta hanyar alurar rigakafi. Duk da haka, akwai wasu cututtuka, ciki har da wasu daga cikin mafiya haɗari, waɗanda ba rigakafinsu. Wuraren da inda masauƙi ya ke da tsafta da aikin kiwon lafiya da ruwa mai kyau na da ƙarancin barazana ga lafiyar matafiya. translations.source: Prevention: Before travel: contact a travel medicine centre or a physician, preferably 4–8 weeks before departure to receive any required vaccinations; read up on local diseases related to your destination; request information on malaria risk and prevention of mosquito bites. This may include purchasing appropriate preventive medication and packing a bednet and insect repellent; get a medical and dental check-up;
translations.reference: Yin rigakafi: Kafin tafiya: tuntuɓi cibiyar magungunan matafiya ko likita, mafi dacewa, makonni 4-8 kafin tafiya don karɓar duk wani rigakafi da ake buƙata; Yi karatu kan cututtukan gida masu alaƙa da wurin da zaka je; nemi bayani game da haɗarin zazzabin cizon sauro da rigakafin cizon sauro. Wannan na iya ƙunsar sayan magani na kariya da tattara sangen gado mai kwarar ƙwari; yi binciken lafiyar jiki da na haƙori;

translations.source: obtain prescriptions for medication according to length of stay and assemble a suitable medical kit; purchase medical insurance with appropriate cover abroad During travel: eat only thoroughly cooked food and drink only bottled or packaged cold drinks. Boil drinking-water if safety is doubtful.
translations.reference: samu takardar magani bisa tsawon zama ka kuma tattara duk magungunan da suka dace; sayi inshorar kiwon lafiya da duk abin da ya dace a ƙasar waje A lokacin tafiya: Ku ci abincin da aka dafa sosai kaɗai da abin sha wanda aka sanya a cikin kwalba mai sanyi. translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided English phrases, aiming for clarity and natural-sounding language. I'll provide a few options where appropriate to reflect different nuances.  I'll also include explanations where helpful.

**English:** suffer from a chronic disease

**Hausa:** *yi fama da cuta mai tsanaki / zubar da cuta mai tsanaki*

*   **yi fama da:**  Means "to suffer from" or "to struggle with."
*   **zubar da:** Also means "to suffer from" but can imply a more severe or ongoing suffering.
*   **cuta mai tsanaki:**  Means "serious disease" or "chronic disease."  "Mai tsanaki" literally means "of severity" or "serious."

**English:** experience illness in the weeks following their return home

**Hausa:** *su samu cuta a makonni bayan dawowansu gida / su ji ciwo a makonni bayan dawowansu gida*

*   **su samu cuta:** "to get sick" or "to experience illness."
*   **su ji ciwo:** "to feel pain/illness"
*   **a makonni bayan:** "in the weeks after"
*   **dawowansu gida:** "their return home"

**English:** received treatment for malaria while travelling

**Hausa:** *an yiwa su magani don malaria yayin tafiya / sun samu magani don malaria yayin tafiya*

*   **an yiwa su magani:** "they received treatment" (passive voice, common in Hausa)
*   **sun samu magani:** "they got treatment" (active voice)
*   **don malaria:** "for malaria"
*   **yayin tafiya:** "while traveling"

**English:** may have been exposed to a serious infectious disease

**Hausa:** *zasu iya kasancewa an shiga cuta mai hatsari / suna iya shiga cuta mai hatsari*

*   **zasu iya kasancewa:** "they might have been" or "they could have been"
*   **suna iya shiga:** "they might enter/get"
*   **an shiga:** "to be exposed to"
*   **cuta mai hatsari:** "dangerous/serious infectious disease." "Mai hatsari" means "dangerous" or "risky."

**English:** spent more than 3 months in a developing country

**Hausa:** *sun zauna fiye da watanni uku a kasa mai tasiri / sun kware fiye da watanni uku a kasa mai tasiri*

*   **sun zauna:** "they lived/stayed"
*   **sun kware:** "they spent time"
*   **fiye da watanni uku:** "more than three months"
*   **a kasa mai tasiri:** "in a developing country." "Mai tasiri" means "developing" or "productive."

**Complete Translation (Combining all phrases):**

*yi fama da cuta mai tsanaki / zubar da cuta mai tsanaki*  *su samu cuta a makonni bayan dawowansu gida / su ji ciwo a makonni bayan dawowansu gida* *an yiwa su magani don malaria yayin tafiya / sun samu magani don malaria yayin tafiya* *zasu iya kasancewa an shiga cuta mai hatsari / suna iya shiga cuta mai hatsari* *sun zauna fiye da watanni uku a kasa mai tasiri / sun kware fiye da watanni uku a kasa mai tasiri*

translations.source: Tuberculosis Overview: TB is caused by bacteria (Mycobacterium tuberculosis) and it most often affects the lungs. TB is spread through the air when people with lung TB cough, sneeze or spit. A person needs to inhale only a few germs to become infected. Every year, 10 million people fall ill with tuberculosis (TB).
translations.reference: Tarin fuka Taƙaitaccen bayani: Ƙwayar cuta mai suna Mycobacterium tuberculosis ce ke haifar da cutar tarin fuka, kuma mafi yawan lokaci tana shafar huhu. Tarin fuka na yaɗuwa ta hanyar iska idan mutane masu ƙwayar cutar a huhunsu suka yi tar, attishawa ko tofar da yawu. Mutum na buƙatar shaƙar ƙwayoyin cutar kaɗan ne kawai kafin ya kamu da ita. A kowace shekara, mutane miliyan 10 ne ke kamuwa da cutar tarin fuka(TB).

translations.source: Despite being a preventable and curable disease, 1.5 million people die from TB each year – making it the world’s top infectious killer. TB is the leading cause of death of people with HIV and also a major contributor to antimicrobial resistance Most of the people who fall ill with TB live in low- and middle-income countries, but TB is present all over the world. About half of all people with TB can be found in 8 countries: Bangladesh, China, India, Indonesia, Nigeria, Pakistan, Philippines and South Africa. About a quarter of the global population is estimated to have been infected with TB bacteria, but most people will not go on to develop TB disease and some will clear the infection.
translations.reference: Duk da kasancewarta cuta da za a iya karewa kuma ana iya warkewa, mutane miliyan 1.5 suna mutuwa daga tarin fuka a kowace shekara - wanda ya sa ta zama babbar cuta mai yaɗuwa da ke kisa a duniya. Tarin fuka na kan gaba a jerin sanadin mutuwar mutane masu cuta mai karyar garkuwar jiki HIV, kuma yana samar da kaso mai yawa na cutuka masu ƙin jin magani Yawancin masu kamuwa da cutar tarin fuka na rayuwa a ƙasashe masu ƙaranci da matsakaicin ƙarfin tattalin arziƙi, amma ana samun TB a ko'ina a duniya. Ana iya samun kusan rabin dukkan masu fama da tarin fuka a kasashe 8: Bangladesh, China, Indiya, Indonesiya, Najeriya, Pakistan, Philippines da Afirka ta Kudu. An ƙiyasta cewa kimanin kaso ɗaya bisa huɗu na al'ummar duniya na kamuwa da ƙwayar cutar TB, sai dai yawancin mutane ba za su kama cutar ta tarin fuka ba, wasu kuma jikinsu na yaƙar cutar.

TB ita ce babban abin da ke haifar da mutuwar mutanen da ke da HIV, kuma ta kasance babban abin da ke haɓaka jinin hana cuta. Kusan kwatar mutanen duniya an kiyasta cewa sun kamu da ƙwayoyin TB, amma mafi yawan mutane ba za su haɓaka cutar TB ba, kuma wasu za su ceto jinin.

translations.source: Those who are infected but not (yet) ill with the disease cannot transmit it. People infected with TB bacteria have a 5–10% lifetime risk of falling ill with TB. Those with compromised immune systems, such as people living with HIV, malnutrition or diabetes, or people who use tobacco, have a higher risk of falling ill. Symptoms: Common symptoms of TB disease include: Prolonged cough, Chest pain, Weakness or fatigue, Weight loss, Fever, Night sweats Often, these symptoms will be mild for many months, thus leading to delays in seeking care and increasing the risk of spreading the infection to others.
translations.reference: Waɗanda ke ɗauke da ƙwayar cutar amma ba su kamu da rashin lafiya ba tukunna ba sa iya yaɗa ta. Mutanen da suka kamu da ƙwayar cutar tarin fuka na da hatsarin tsawon rayuwa na kaso 5 zuwa 10 na kamuwa da cutar ta TB. Mutanen da ke da raunin garkuwar jiki, kamar mutanen da ke rayuwa da cuta mai karya garkuwar jiki wato HIV, ƙaranci abinci mai gina jiki ko ciwon suga, ko masu shan sigari, sun fi zama cikin hatsarin yin rashin lafiyar. Alamomi: Alamomin cutar tarin fuka sun haɗa da: Taro na lokaci mai tsawo, Ciwon ƙirji, Kasala ko gajiya, Ramewa, Zazzaɓi, Gumin dare. Mafi yawan lokuta, waɗannan alamomi sukan kasance marasa tsanani na tsawon watanni, wanda hakan ke haifar da jinkiri wajen neman lafiya da kuma ƙara hatsarin yaɗa cutar ga wasu.

translations.source: If the healthcare provider suspects a patient to have TB disease, they will send the patient for testing. In the case of suspected lung TB disease, patients will be asked to give a sputum sample for testing for TB bacteria. For non-lung TB disease, samples of affected body fluids and tissue can be tested. WHO recommends rapid molecular diagnostic tests as initial tests for people showing signs and symptoms of TB. Other diagnostic tools can include sputum smear microscopy and chest X-rays.
translations.reference: Idan ma'aikacin lafiya ya yi zargin mara lafiya na fama a cutar tarin fuka, zai aika wannan marar lafiyar domin yin gwaji. Ta ɓangaren cutar tarin fuka da ta shafi huhu, za a buƙaci mara lafiya da ya ba da yawunsa don a yi gwajin cutar ta tarin fuka. Ga tarin fuka da bai shafi huhu ba kuma, ana iya gwada samfurin ruwan jiki ko ko wani ɓangare da cutar ta shafa. WHO na ba da shawara yin gwajin gano ƙwayoyin cuta cikin sauri a matsayin gwajin farko ga mutanen da ke nuna alamun kamuwa da tarin fuka. Sauran hanyoyin gwaje-gwajen na iya haɗawa da duba yawu a ƙarƙashin madubin binciken ƙwaƙwaf da kuma hoton ƙirji.

translations.source: With TB infection, a person gets infected with TB bacteria that lie inactive in the body. This infection can develop into TB disease if their immune system weakens. People with TB infection do not show any signs or symptoms of TB. To identify TB infection, healthcare providers will screen at-risk patients to rule out active TB, and they may use a skin or blood test to check for TB infection. Treatment: TB disease is curable.
translations.reference: Wajen kamuwa da tarin fuka, mutum na gamuwa da ƙwayar cutar tarin fuka wacce ta zama a jiki ba tare da wani tasiri ba. Kamuwa da wannan ƙwayar cutar na iya juyewa ya zama cikakkiyar cutar tarin fuka idan garkuwar jikinsu ta samu rauni. Ta yiwu mutanen da suka kamu da ƙwayar cutar tarin fuka ba za su nuna wasu alamomin cutar ta tarin fuka ba. Don gano ko an kamu da cutar tarin fuka, ma'aikatan lafiya za su duba marasa lafiya da ke cikin haɗari don kawar da yiwuwar kamuwa da tarin fuka, kuma suna iya amfani da gwajin fata ko jini don bincikar tarin fuka. Magani: Tarin fuka cuta ce da ake iya warkewa.

translations.source: It is treated by standard 6 month course of 4 antibiotics. Common drugs include rifampicin and isoniazid. In some cases the TB bacteria does not respond to the standard drugs. In this case, the patient has drug-resistant TB. for drug-resistant TB is longer and more complex.
translations.reference: Ana magance ta ta hanyar shan daidaitattun magunguna kala 4 na tsawon wata 6. Magungunan da ake yawan amfani da su sun haɗa da rifampicin da isoniazid. Amma wasu lokutan cutar tarin fuka ba ta karɓar daidaitattun magungunan. A wannan yanayin, mara lafiyan yana fama da tarin fuka da ba ya jin magani. Tarin fuka da ba ya jin magani na ɗaukar lokaci mai tsawo kuma yana da sarƙaƙiya.
translations.hypothesis:

**Hausa Translation:**

Ana jinya da abubuwan da suka haɗa da kasancewa a kan allurar antibiotics na watanni 6. Misalan kasuwanci sun haɗa da rifampicin da isoniazid. A wasu lokuta, kwayar cutar TB ba ta amsa ga abubuwan da ke aiki yadda ya kamata. A wannan yanayin, mai haƙuri yana da TB mai juriya ga miƙa shaida. Jinya ga TB mai juriya ga miƙa shaida yana da tsauri da kuma tsawaita.

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"It is treated"**:  "Ana jinya da" - This translates to "It is being treated with..."  It's a common way to express treatment in Hausa.
*   **"standard 6 month course"**: "kasancewa a kan allurar antibiotics na watanni 6" - This conveys the idea of a standard course of antibiotics lasting 6 months.
*   **"Common drugs include"**: "Misalan kasuwanci sun haɗa da" - "Common examples include" is a natural way to introduce examples of drugs.
*   **"rifampicin and isoniazid"**: These are transliterated as they are drug names.
*   **"TB bacteria does not respond"**: "kwayar cutar TB ba ta amsa" - "The TB bacteria does not respond."
*   **"drug-resistant TB"**: "TB mai juriya ga miƙa shaida" - "TB with resistance to medication."
*   **"longer and more complex"**: "yana da tsauri da kuma tsawaita" - "It is more difficult and longer."

**Important Notes for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  Without more context about the document's purpose, this is the best general translation.  If it's for a specific audience (e.g., medical professionals, general public), the language could be adjusted further.

translations.source: The course of TB drugs is provided to the patient with information, supervision and support by a health worker or trained volunteer. Without such support, treatment adherence can be difficult. If the treatment is not properly completed, the disease can become drug-resistant and can spread. In the case of TB infection (where the patient is infected with TB bacteria but not ill), TB preventive treatment can be given to stop the onset of disease. This treatment uses the same drugs for a shorter time.
translations.reference: Ana ba wa marar lafiyar maganin tarin fuka da yake buƙata tare da bayanai da kuma sa'ido da tallafi daga ma'aikacin lafiya ko wani mai aikin sa kai da aka horas. Idan babu tallafi, tabbatar da an kammala shan maganin na iya zama abu mai wahala. Idan ba a kammala shan maganin yadda ya kamata ba, cutar na iya juyewa zuwa wacce ba ta jin magani kuma ta yaɗu. Game da kamuwa da ƙwayar cutar tarin fuka kuwa (ta yadda mara lafiyar ya kamu da ƙwayar cutar amma bai fara rashin lafiya ba), ana iya ba shi maganin kariya daga cutar tarin fuka don hana bayyanar alamomin cutar. Maganin iri ɗaya ake sha da na magance cutar, amma na lokacin da bai kai wancan tsawo ba.

translations.source: Recent treatment options have shortened the duration to treatment to only 1 or 3 months, as compared to 6 months in the past.
translations.reference: Zaɓuɓɓukan magani na baya-bayan nan sun ƙara gajarce tsawon lokacin shan maganin zuwa wata 1 ko 3 kawai, idan aka kwatanta da watanni 6 da akan yi a baya.
translations.hypothesis:

*   **Sababbin hanyoyin jiyya:** "Recent treatment options" -  "Sababi" means recent/new, "hanyoyin" means methods/ways, and "jiyya" means treatment.
*   **sun rage lokacin jiyya:** "have shortened the duration of treatment" - "sun rage" means "they have reduced/shortened," "lokacin" means "duration/time," and "jiyya" again means treatment.
*   **zuwa kasa da wata ɗaya ko uku:** "to only 1 or 3 months" - "zuwa" means "to," "kasa da" means "less than/below," "wata ɗaya" means "one month," "ko" means "or," and "uku" means "three."
*   **a kwatance da watanni shida da suka gabata:** "as compared to 6 months in the past" - "a kwatance da" means "in comparison to/as compared to," "watanni shida" means "six months," and "da suka gabata" means "that were in the past/previously."

I've aimed for a clear and natural-sounding Hausa translation while maintaining accuracy to the original English meaning.

translations.source: Ultraviolet radiation Overview: Ultraviolet (UV) radiation covers the wavelength range of 100–400 nm, which is a higher frequency and lower wavelength than visible light. UV radiation comes naturally from the sun, but it can also be created by artificial sources used in industry, commerce and recreation. The UV region covers the wavelength range 100-400 nm and is divided into three bands: UVA (315-400 nm)UVB (280-315 nm)UVC (100-280 nm). As sunlight passes through the atmosphere, all UVC and approximately 90% of UVB radiation is absorbed by ozone, water vapour, oxygen and carbon dioxide.
translations.reference: Hasken Ultraviolet radiation Taƙaitaccen bayani: Ultraviolet radiation (UV) na nufin hasken da ya faɗo a ma'aunin haske na 100-400nm, wanda ya fi ƙarfi kuma yake da ƙarancin nisan zango fiye da da hasken da ake iya gani da ido. UV radiation na samuwa ne daga rana, amma kuma ana iya ƙirƙirarsa ta hanyoyin da ɗan adam ya samar waɗanda ake amfani da su a masana'antu, kasuwanci da motsa jiki. Yankin UV ya ƙunshi ma'aunin haske 100-400 nm kuma an raba shi zuwa rukuni uku: UVA (315-400 nm) UVB (280-315 nm) UVC (100-280 nm). Yayin da hasken rana ke wuce sararin samaniya, shimfiɗar sama, tururin ruwa, iskar oxygen da ta carbon dioxide na shanye duk hasken da ke rukunin UVC da kuma kaso 90% na wanda ke rukunin UVB.

Tabbas na fitar UV yana fitowa ta hanyar rana ta halita, amma kuma za'a iya samar da shi ta hanyar kusan abubuwan da aka ƙirƙira a cikin masana'antu, kasuwanci da nishaɗi.  Yayin da rana ke fitar da haske, duk tabbas na fitar UVC da kusan 90% na tabbas na fitar UVB an shafe su ne ta hanyar ozone, ruwan iska, iska da carbon dioxide.  Yayin da haske na rana ke shude ta sararin samaniya, duk tabbas na fitar UVC da kusan 90% na tabbas na fitar UVB an shafe su ne ta hanyar ozone, ruwan iska, iska da carbon dioxide.  Yawanin fitar UV yana kunshe da juyin haske na 100-400 nm kuma an rarrake shi zuwa sashoshi uku: UVA (315-400 nm) UVB (280-315 nm) UVC (100-280 nm).

translations.source: UVA radiation is less affected by the atmosphere. Therefore, the UV radiation reaching the Earth’s surface is largely composed of UVA with a small UVB component. The amount of UV radiation from the sun that hits the Earth’s surface depends on several factors, including the sun’s height in the sky, latitude, cloud cover, altitude, the thickness of the ozone layer and ground reflection. Reductions in the ozone layer due to human-created pollution increase the amount of UVA and UVB that reaches the surface. This can impact human health, animals, marine organisms and plant life.
translations.reference: Hasken rukunin UVA bai cika tasirantuwa da sinadaran da ke sararin samaniya ba. Dun haka, hasken UV da ke isowa duniya yawanci ya ƙunshi UVA ne da kaɗan daga UVB. Adadin hasken UV radiation daga rana da ke isowa duniya ya dogara da abubuwa da yawa, ciki har da tsayin rana a sararin sama, nisa ta ɓangaren latitude, kaurin gajimare, nisan altitude, kaurin shimfiɗar sararin samaniya ta ozone da yanayin ƙasa. Rage kauri da shimfiɗar sararin samaniya ta ozone ta yi saboda gurɓacewar yanayi da ɗan adam ya haifar yana ƙara adadin UVA da UVB waɗanda ke isowa duniya. Wannan na iya yin tasiri ga lafiyar ɗan adam, dabbobi, halittun ruwa da rayuwar shuka.

Ragowar layer ɗin ozone sakamakon cutarwa da mutane suka haifu ya ƙara adadin UVA da UVB da ke kaiwa kasashen Duniya. translations.source: In humans, increased UV exposure can cause skin cancers, cataracts and immune system damage. Prevention: The rise in the number of cases of skin cancer over the past decades is strongly related to increased exposure to the sun during outdoor activities and to artificial sources of UV radiation such as sunlamps and tanning beds. Overexposure is also the underlying cause of harmful effects on the eyes and immune system. Adopting a few simple precautions can greatly reduce the risk of these health conditions: Limit time in the midday sun from 10 a.m. to 4 p.m., particularly on days when the UV index is high When UV rays are most intense and you must be outside, try to find shade and wear protective clothing, including a brimmed hat that provides sun protection for your head, face and neck.
translations.reference: A cikin mutane, ƙari a haske UV na iya haifar da ciwon sankarar fata, amosanin ido da karya garkuwar jiki. Kariya: ƙari a yawan masu kamuwa da cutar kansar fata a cikin gomman shekarun da suka gabata yana da alaƙa mai ƙarfi da ƙarin shiga rana yayin ayyukan waje da kuma hanyoyin samar da hasken UV da ɗan adam ya samar kamar fitilu masu haske kamar rana da gadajen sauya launin fata. Har ila yau, amfani da ya wuce gona da iri shi ne sanadin cutar da idanu da gaskuwar jiki. Ɗaukar wasu 'yan matakan na kiyayewa na iya rage hatsarin kamuwa da illolin na lafiya: Iyakance lokaci shiga tsakar rana daga 10 na safe zuwa 4 na yamma, musamman a ranakun da ma'aunin UV ya yi sama Lokacin da hasken UV ya fi tsanani kuma dole sai ka fita waje, yi ƙoƙari ku nemo gilashin kare rana kuma ka sanya tufafin kariya, gami da hula mai malafa da za ta ba da kariya daga rana ga kai, fuska da wuyanka.

translations.source: Use a broad-spectrum sunscreen of at least 15 SPF – ideally higher– and reapply every 2 hours. Avoid sunlamps and tanning parlours, as they are known to damage the skin and eyes. Children’s UV exposure should be carefully limited because they are in a dynamic state of growth and therefore more susceptible to environmental threats than adults. Many vital functions such as the immune system are not fully developed at birth, and unsafe environments may interfere with their normal development. WHO Response: WHO works with Member States and partners to increase public understanding of the effects of overexposure to ultraviolet radiation.
translations.reference: Yi amfani da man shiga rana na aƙalla ajin 15 SPF - ko wanda ya fi shi - kuma a sake shafa bayan duk awa 2. Gujewa amfani da fitilu masu haske irin na rana da wuraren sauya launin fata, domin an san suna lalata fata da idanu. A taƙaita shigar yara hasken UV saboda suna wani mataki mai muhimmanci na girma don haka sun fi manya saurin fuskantar matsala daga ababe masu hatsari a muhalli. Gaɓoɓi masu muhimmanci, kamar garkuwar jikin ɗan adam ba su kammalu ba yayin da ake haihuwar mutum, don haka muhalli mara aminci na iya kawo cikas a ci gabansu kamar yadda ya dace. Martani daga Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya: Hukumar na aiki da ƙasashen da ke ƙarƙashinta da kuma abokan hulɗarta don ƙara wayar da kai game da hatsarin da ke tattare da yawan ta'ammali da haske ultraviolet radiation.

Kyakkyawar kariya ta jikin rana mai ƙarfi (sunscreen) taƙaƙan 15 SPF – mafi kyau idan ya fi – kuma sake aikowa duk sa'o biyu. Ka guje wa fitattun haske na rana (sunlamps) da wuraren da ake daidaita jikin rana, domin an san su na lalata jini da ido. Ya kamata a kulle yawan kashen rana ga yara sosai, domin suna cikin wadataccen ci gaba kuma saboda haka, sun fi kasala ga barazanun yanayi fiye da manya. Ayyukan da suka fi muhimmanci da yawa, irin su tsarin jinin, ba su cikakken bayan hankali a haihuwa ba, kuma yanayi mara amanci na iya tsoma baki da ci gaba da al'ada. translations.source: The INTERSUN Programme is a collaboration between WHO, the United Nations Environment Programme, the World Meteorological Organization, the International Agency on Cancer Research and the International Commission on Non-Ionizing Radiation Protection. It promotes and evaluates research on the health effects of UV radiation, and develops an appropriate response through guidelines, recommendations and information dissemination. The goals of the programme including providing practical and sound advice on the health and environmental impacts of UV exposure, to encourage countries to take action to reduce UV-induced health risks and to provide guidance to national authorities about sun awareness programmes. To this end, the programme collaborates with experts and specialist agencies to implement key research activities, identifies and quantifies health risks from UV radiation, develops reliable predictions of health and environmental consequences of changes in UV exposure with stratospheric ozone depletion, and develops practical ways of monitoring change in UV-induced health effects over time. The programme provides tools and guidelines related to the UV index, sun protection for children, artificial sunbeds, tourism and occupational health.
translations.reference: Shirin INTERSUN shiri ne na haɗin gwiwa tsakanin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya, Shirin Muhalli na Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya, Ƙungiyar Lura da Sararin Samaniya ta World Meteorological Organization, cibiyar binciken da ya shafi cutar sankara ta International Agency on Cancer Research da kuma hukumar International Commission on Non-Ionizing Radiation Protection. Yana ƙarfafawa da tantance bincike a kan UV radiation ga lafiya, kuma yana samar da hanyoyin agaji da suka dace ta hanyar samar da jagora, shawarwari da yaɗa bayanai. Manufar shirin ta haɗa da bayar da shawarwari masu inganci kan illar da hasken UV ke da shi ga lafiya da muhalli, karfafa gwiwar ƙasashe su ɗauki matakin rage illa ga lafiya da UV ke haifarwa da kuma samar da tsari ga mahukuntan ƙasa game da shirye-shiryen wayar da kan hasken rana. Don cimma wannan manufar, shirin yana haɗa gwiwa da masana da ƙwararrun hukumomi wajen aiwatar da muhimman ayyukan bincike, ganowa da ƙididdige haɗarin UV radiation ga lafiya, samar da ingantaccen hasashe game da illar da ke tattare da canji a yawan ta'ammali da hasken UV ga lafiya da muhalli, da lalata sararin samaniya, da samar da hanyoyin da za a iya amfani da su wajen saka ido kan canje-canje masu tasiri kan lafiya da hasken UV ke haifarwa bayan tsawon lokaci. Shirin yana samar da kayan aiki da shawarwari da suka danganci ma'aunin UV, kariya daga hasken rana ga yara, gadaje masu fitar da haske irin na rana, yawon shaƙatawa da tabbatar da lafiya yayin gudanar aiki ko sana'a.

Tana haɓaka da ƙima bincike kan tasirin da radiation na UV ke yi wa lafiya, kuma tana ƙirƙirar amsar da ta dace ta hanyar umurnai, shawara, da yaduwar bayanai.  Makammar wannan shirin ta haɗa da ba da shawara mai amfani da kuma mai ƙarfi kan tasirin lafiya da muhittin da radiation na UV ke yi, don haɓaka ƙwarewar kasashe don ɗaukar mataki don rage haɗarin lafiya da ke tasirinsa daga UV, da kuma ba da jagora ga hukumomin kasa kan shirye-shiryen wayar da hankali game da rana. Shirin yana ba da kayan aiki da umurnai da suka danganci kundin UV, kariya daga rana ga yara, manyan sunbeds na sunan, yawon bude ido, da lafiya a wurin aiki.

translations.source: Universal health coverage Overview: Universal health coverage (UHC) means that all people have access to the full range of quality health services they need, when and where they need them, without financial hardship. It covers the full continuum of essential health services, from health promotion to prevention, treatment, rehabilitation and palliative care. To deliver on this promise, countries need to have strong, efficient and equitable health systems that are rooted in the communities they serve. Primary health care (PHC) is the most effective and cost-efficient way to get there.
translations.reference: Gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya a faɗin duniya wato Universal Health Coverage Taƙaitaccen bayani: Universal Health Coverage (UHC) wato samar da gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya a faɗin duniya na nufin cewa kowanne mutum na da damar amfani da cikakku kuma ingantattun ayyukan lafiya da suke buƙata, a lokacin da suke buƙata kuma a wajen da suke buƙatarsu, ba tare da matsalar rashin kuɗi ba. Ya haɗa da cikakkun ayyukan lafiya na tilas, daga wayar da kai zuwa kariya, magance cuta, gyaran hali da kula da masu matsanantan cututtuka. Domin cika wannan alƙawarin, akwai buƙatar ƙasashe su kasance suna da tsarin kiwon lafiya da yake da ƙarfi, yake aiki kuma suke aiki ga kowa ba tare da wariya ba, masu tushe a cikin al'ummar da suke yi wa aiki. Kula da lafiya matakin farko ne hanya mafi aiki kuma mai arha wajen samar da wannan tsari na kiwon lafiya.

translations.source: Every country has a different path to achieving UHC and to decide what to cover based on the needs of their populations and the resources at hand. Investing in PHC ensures that all those needs are identified, prioritized and addressed in an integrated way; that there is a robust and equipped health and care workforce; and that all sectors of society contribute to confronting the environmental and socio-economic factors that affect health and well-being, including preparing for, responding to and recovering from emergencies. WHO response: As a foundation for UHC, WHO recommends reorienting health systems using a primary health care (PHC) approach. Achieving UHC is a WHO strategic priority, with the goal of 1 billion more people benefitting from universal health coverage by 2025. In countries with fragile health systems, we focus on technical assistance to build national institutions and service delivery to fill critical gaps in emergencies.
translations.reference: Kowace ƙasa na da hanya ta daban ta cimma gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya su kuma yanke shawarar abin da za su yi ta la'akari da buƙatun al'ummarsu da kuma kayan aikin da suke da su. Sanya jari a Matakin Farko na Kula da Lafiya na tabbatar da cewa an gane dukkan waɗannan buƙatu, an ba su muhimmanci kuma an warware su ta hanyar da ta haɗa da kowane ɓangare; an samar da ma'aikatan lafiya wadatattu masu sanin makamar aiki; kuma dukkan ɓangarori a al'umma suna ba da gudummuwa wajen fuskantar ƙalubalen muhalli da tattalin arziƙi da ke shafar lafiya da walwala, wanda ya haɗa da shirya wa, kai agaji da kuma farfaɗowa da buƙatun lafiya da kan taso cikin gaggawa. Martanin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya: A matsayin wani tushe na samar da Gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya (UHC) Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya na ba da shawarar sake saita tsare-tsaren kula da lafiya ta amfani da tsarin kula da lafiya a matakin farko (PHC). Tabbatar da samuwar gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya (UHC) shiri ne mai muhimmanci na Hukumar Kula da Lafiya ta duniya, da burin samun ƙarin mutane biliyan 1 da za su ci moriyar tsarin kula da lafiya na duniya a shekarar 2025. A ƙasashe masu tsarin kula da lafiya mai rauni, muna mayar da hankali kan samar da taimakon dabarun aiki don gina hukumomi na ƙasa da ayyukan da za su cike muhimman gurabe a lokacin buƙatun gaggawa.

translations.source: In more robust health system settings, we drive public health impact towards health coverage for all through policy dialogue for the systems of the future and strategic support to improve performance. This work is supported by normative guidance and agreements; data, research and innovation; and leadership in the realms of diplomacy, advocacy, gender equality, health equity and human rights, multisectoral action, and finance. 2030 Agenda for SDGs WHO’s work is aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDG) target 3.8, which focuses on achieving universal health coverage, including financial risk protection, access to quality essential health-care services and access to safe, effective, quality and affordable essential medicines and vaccines for all. Globally, progress towards universal health coverage is a challenge. Progress in service coverage has stalled while the proportion of the population facing catastrophic out-of-pocket health spending increases continuously.
translations.reference: A wuraren da ke da tsarin kula da lafiya tsayayye, muna ƙarfafar samar da tasirin kula da lafiyar al'umma ta ɓangaren faɗaɗa aikin ga kowa ta hanyar samar da dokiki don samar da tsare-tsaren da za a ci moriya nan gaba da kuma ba da muhimmin tallafi wajen inganta aiki. Wannan aiki ana yinsa bisa bin tsarin aiwatarwa da aka saba da kuma yarjejeniyoyi; bayanai, bincike da ƙirƙira; da shugabanci a fagen diflomasiyya, wayar da kai, daidaiton jinsi, wadatar kula da lafiya da haƙƙoƙin al'umma, ayyukan da suka shafi ɓangarori da dama, da kuma hada-hadar kuɗi. Ajandar 2030 ta aikace-aikacen Hukumar Kula da Lafiya ta Duniya ta dace da Manufofin Ci gaba Mai Ɗorewa (SDG'S) na 3.8 wanda yake mayar da hankali wajen samar da gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya a duniya, wanda ya haɗa da ba da kariya ga marasa ƙarfin tattalin arziƙi, damar amfani da ingantattun ayyukan kula da lafiya da kuma damar amfani da magangunan da alluran rigakafi da ake buƙata masu aminci, da suke aiki, ingantattu kuma masu sauƙin saye ga kowa. A duniya bakiɗaya, ci gaba a fannin samar da gamammen tsarin kula da lafiya abu ne da ke fuskantar ƙalubale. Ci gaba ta fannin adadin mutanen da ke amfana da ayyukan lafiya ya samu tsaiko yayin da adadin al'ummar da ke fuskantar mummunan kashe kuɗi don neman lafiya ke ci gaba da ƙaruwa.

**Hausa Translation:**

A cikin tsarin lafiya na ƙarfi, muna haɓaka tasirin lafiya ta jama'a zuwa kiyayyar lafiya ga kowa ta hanyar tattaunawar manufa don tsarin gaba da gaba da tallafin ƙwarin gwiwa don inganta aikin.  Ana tallafawa wannan aikin ne ta hanyar jagorancin ƙa'ida da yarjejeniyoyi; bayanan, bincike da sabbin abubuwan; da kuma jagorancin fannin dIPLOMASI, shawara, daidaito da jima'i, kiyayyar lafiya, hakkokin bil'adi, aikin da aka shirya a kowane fanni, da kudi.  Agenda 2030 don SDGs Aikin WHO ya haɗu da burin ci gaban kimiya ta dabi'a (SDG) na 3.8, wanda ke mai da hankali kan cimma kiyayyar lafiya ta kowa, gami da kiyaye hada mutane daga kashe kudi akan lafiya, samun isasshen damamn da aiki akan ayyukan lafiya na daidaito da bukata, da samun isasshen damamn da aiki akan samar da magani da allurar cuta na daidaito da bukata, da kuma farin ciki. A duniya, ci gaba zuwa kiyayyar lafiya ta kowa yana da ƙalubale. Ci gaba a cikin hidima ya tsaya a wuri guda, yayin da yawan mutane da ke fuskantar kashe kudi akan lafiya ya ƙara ne da ƙarfi.

**Notes on Translation Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"Robust health system settings"**: Translated as "A cikin tsarin lafiya na ƙarfi" (In a strong/robust health system).
*   **"Public health impact"**: Translated as "tasirin lafiya ta jama'a" (public health impact).
*   **"Policy dialogue"**: Translated as "tattaunawar manufa" (policy discussion/dialogue).
*   **"Strategic support"**: Translated as "tallafin ƙwarin gwiwa" (support with strength/strategic support).
*   **"Normative guidance"**: Translated as "jagorancin ƙa'ida" (guidance based on rules/norms).
*   **"Diplomacy, advocacy, gender equality, health equity and human rights"**: These were translated directly, maintaining the order and meaning as closely as possible.
*   **"Multisectoral action"**: Translated as "aiki da aka shirya a kowane fanni" (action organized in every sector).
*   **"Sustainable Development Goals (SDG)"**:  Kept as "Ci gaban kimiya ta dabi'a (SDG)" - using the common Hausa translation for Sustainable Development Goals.
*   **"Universal health coverage"**: Translated as "kiyayyar lafiya ta kowa" (health coverage for everyone).
*   **"Financial risk protection"**: Translated as "kiyaye hada mutane daga kashe kudi akan lafiya" (protecting people from spending money on health).
*   **"Catastrophic out-of-pocket health spending"**: Translated as "kashe kudi akan lafiya" (spending money on health).
*   **Word Choice:** I tried to use common and easily understandable Hausa words.
*   **Sentence Structure:** I adjusted the sentence structure slightly to flow more naturally in Hausa while preserving the original meaning.

translations.source: WHO monitors progress and publishes global reports to reveal the latest evidence every two years. See the reports here and visit the UHC fact sheet for more information.
translations.reference: Hukumar Lafiya ta duniya ta ci gaba da sa ido kan ci gaban tana kuma wallafa rahoto game da halin da duniya ke ciki don bayyana shaida mafi sabunta bayan duk shekara biyu. Duba rahotannin a nan kuma ka ziyarci shafin sahihin bayani game da Gamammen Tsarin Kula da lafiya (UHC) don ƙarin bayani.

**Explanation of Choices & Nuances:**

*   **WHO:**  We keep "WHO" as is, as it's a well-known acronym.
*   **ta duba ci gaba da aikin:** "ta duba" means "monitors" or "reviews." "ci gaba da aikin" means "progress of the work/effort."

*   **duk shekarar biyu:** "duk" means "every." "shekarar biyu" means "two years."

*   **kuma ziyarci shafin bayani na UHC don karin bayani:** "kuma" means "and." "ziyarci" means "visit." "shafin bayani" means "fact sheet/information page." "na UHC" means "of UHC." "don karin bayani" means "for more information."

**Important Notes for Low-Resource Languages:**

translations.source: Urban health Overview: Urbanization is one of the leading global trends of the 21st century that has a significant impact on health. Over 55% of the world’s population live in urban areas – a proportion that is expected to increase to 68% by 2050. As most future urban growth will take place in developing countries, the world today has a unique opportunity to guide urbanization and other major urban development trends in a way that protects and promotes health. This is important, not least because the health and well-being of citizens is perhaps a city’s most important asset.
translations.reference: Kula da lafiya a birane Taƙaitaccen bayani: Faɗaɗar birani na ɗaya daga cikin ababen na kan gaba da ke tashe a duniya a ƙarni na 21 wanda ke da tasiri sosai a kan lafiya. Sama sa kaso 55 da al'ummar duniya na rayuwa a birane - ana sa rai wannan adadin zai ƙaru zuwa kaso 68 nan da 2050. Da yake faɗaɗa birane da zai zo nan gaba zai auku ne a ƙasashe masu tasowa, duniyar a yau tana da wata dama ta musamman ta tsara yadda faɗaɗar biranen za ta kasance da ma sauran manyan ababen da suka shafi ci gaban birane a wata hanya da za ta ba da kariya ta kuma ƙarfafi kiwon lafiya. Wannan yana da muhimmanci, musamman da yake lafiya da walwalar al'umma na iya zama kadara mafi daraja ta kowanne birni.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided text, aiming for clarity and accuracy. I've included some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Hausa Translation:**

Kwamancen Lafiya a Birane: Karuwar yawan jama'a (Urbanization) daya daga cikin manyan abubuwan da ke faruwa a duniya a wannan karni na 21, kuma tana tasiri sosai kan lafiya. Sama da kashi 55% na mutanen duniya suna zaune a birane – kashi da ake sa ran zai tashi zuwa kashi 68% a shekarar 2050.  Domin yawancin karuwar biranen da za ta faru a nan gaba za ta kasance a kasashen da ke tasowa, duniya ta samu damar waccan banza ta jagoranci karuwar birane da sauran manyan ayyukan da ke faruwa a birane, ta hanyar kare lafiya da kuma bunkasuwa. Wannan ya zama wajibi, musamman saboda lafiyar mata da maza da kuma koshin lafiya na mazauna birane, na daya daga cikin mafi muhimmanci abubuwan da birnin ke da shi.

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **"Urban health Overview"**: Translated as "Kwamancen Lafiya a Birane" (Health Matters/Aspects in Urban Areas).  "Kwamancen" is a good general term for "overview" or "aspects" in this context.
*   **"Urbanization"**: Translated as "Karuwar yawan jama'a" (Increase in population/urban growth). This is a common and understandable way to express urbanization in Hausa.
*   **"Significant impact"**: Translated as "tana tasiri sosai" (it has a very strong effect).
*   **"Developing countries"**: Translated as "kasashen da ke tasowa" (developing countries).
*   **"Guide urbanization"**: Translated as "jagoranci karuwar birane" (to lead urban growth).
*   **"Health and well-being"**: Translated as "lafiyar mata da maza da kuma koshin lafiya" (health of men and women and also well-being/good health).  Hausa often uses a slightly more explicit phrasing for "well-being."
*   **"City’s most important asset"**: Translated as "mafi muhimmanci abubuwan da birnin ke da shi" (the most important things a city has).

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  Hausa has regional variations. This translation aims for a generally understandable form.
*   **Terminology:**  Technical terms related to urban planning and public health might need further refinement depending on the specific audience.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:** Ideally, this translation should be reviewed by a native Hausa speaker, especially someone familiar with the subject matter, to ensure accuracy and naturalness.

translations.source: However, most of the 4.2 billion people living in cities suffer inadequate housing and transport, poor sanitation and waste management, and air quality that fails WHO guidelines. Other forms of pollution, such as noise, water and soil contamination, so-called urban heat islands, and a lack of space for walking, cycling and active living further combine to make cities epicentres of a noncommunicable disease epidemic and drivers of climate change. WHO Repository on Urban Health Impact: Around 40% of urban growth is in slums that lack safe water and sanitation, and 91% of people in urban areas breathe polluted air. When it comes to healthy diets, urbanization increases the distance from farm to fork, driving demand for unhealthy, processed foods.
translations.reference: Sai dai, da yawa daga cikin mutane biliyan 4.2 da ke rayuwa a birane na fama da matsalar ƙarancin muhalli da hanyoyin sufuri, rashin tsaftar muhalli da sarrafa shara, da kuma ingancin iskar shaƙa wanda bai cika sharuɗɗan da hukumar lafiya ta duniya ta bayar ba. Sauran nau'ukan gurɓatar yanayi, kamar su hayaniya, ruwa da guɓacewar ƙasa, da wuraren da ake kira tsibiran zafi na birane, da rashin sarari don yin tafiyar ƙafa, tuƙa keke da kuma rayuwa mai cike da kuzari sun haɗu sun sanya biranen zama cibiyar annobar cutukan da ba sa yaɗuwa da kuma haddasa sauyin yanayi. Ma'ajiyar Bayanai ta Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya game da Kiwon Lafiya a Birane Tasiri: Kusan kaso 40 na faɗaɗar birane na faruwa ne a unguwannin talakawa da ba su da ruwa mai tsafta da tsaftar muhalli, kuma kaso 91 na mutanen da ke zaune a birane na shaƙar gurɓatacciyar iska. Idan ana batun abinci mai lafiya, faɗaɗar birane na ƙara nesanta mutane da gonaki wanda ke ƙara ƙarfafar buƙatar abincin da aka sarrafa wanda ba shi da kyau ga jiki.
