translations.source: engage more with civil society; and work with Member States to strengthen incentives for collaboration through political leadership, governance direction and funding to support collaboration. Key findings of the report will feed into discussions of the next United Nations General Assembly SDG Summit and the High-level Meeting on Universal Health Coverage in September 2023. The months leading up to these key events provide an opportunity for a joint push by Member States and multilateral agencies to identify ways on how to implement the recommendations and to prepare the ground to fully leverage stronger collaboration and accelerate progress towards the health-related SDGs in the second half of the SDG timeframe. Note to the editor:
translations.reference: Asusun Yara na Majalisar Dinkin Duniya (UNICEF); Unitaid; Aiki tare da mambobin ƙasashe don ƙarfafa gwiwa ta hanyar jagoranci a siyasance da kuma jagoranci na shugabanci da kudi domin tallafawa da haɗin gwiwa. Mihimman binciken da aka samu na rahoton da aka samu na rahoton zai bada labari game da tattaunawar babban taron majalisar dinkin duniya na cigaban muradan ƙarni SDG da kuma taron duniya na akan kiwon lafiya a watan Satumbar 2023. Watanni kafin waɗannan muhimman abubuwan da suka faru sun bada dama ga haɗin gwiwar ƙasashe membobi da hukumomi da ƙungiyoyi daban daban don gano hanyoyin da za’a aiwatar da shawarwarin da shiryawa don yin ƙarfaffan haɗin gwiwa tare da haɓaka cigaba ga cigaban muradan ƙarni masu alaƙa da kiwon lafiya na rabin lokacin da SDG ta dauka. translations.source: The SDG3 GAP describes how the 13 signatory agencies will adopt new ways of working, building on existing successful collaborations, and jointly align their support around national plans and strategies that are country-owned and -led. The signatories to the SDG3 GAP are Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance; Global Financing Facility for Women, Children and Adolescents (GFF); Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria (Global Fund); International Labour Organization (ILO);
translations.reference: SDG GAP ya bayyana yadda hukumomi goma sha da suka sahannu zasu yi amfani da sabbin hanyoyin aiki, tare. Masu rattaba hannu ga ciga cigaban muradan ƙarni SDG3 GAP sune Gavi, da kuma ƙungiyar allurar rigakafi Kayan Tallafi na Duniya don Mata, Yara da Matasa (GFF); Asusun duniya don yaƙi da cutar AIDS,tarin fuka da zazzaɓin cizon sauro(Asusun duniya) Ƙungiyar ƙwadago ta duniya (ILO);

Mai saita dokokin SDG3 GAP su ne: Gavi, Kungiyar Kariya ta Kwayoyin Cutacce; Kayan Aikin Tallafin Kudi na Duniya don Mata, Yara da Matasa (GFF); Kungiyar Duniya don Yaƙi da HIV/AIDS, Tuberkulosis da Malaria (Global Fund); Kungiyar Aiki ta Kasa (ILO);

translations.source: New survey results show health systems starting to recover from pandemic After three years of the COVID-19 pandemic, health systems in countries have started showing the first major signs of health system recovery, according to the WHO interim report on the “Fourth round of the global pulse survey on continuity of essential health services during the COVID-19 pandemic: November 2022–January 2023”. By early 2023, countries reported experiencing reduced disruptions in the delivery of routine health services, but highlighted the need to invest in recovery and stronger resilience for the future. Among the 139 countries that responded to the fourth round of WHO’s pulse survey, countries reported continued disruptions in almost one-quarter of services on average. In 84 countries where trend analysis is possible, the percentage of disrupted services declined on average from 56% in July-September 2020 to 23% in November 2022- January 2023.
translations.reference: Sabon sakamakon binciken ya nuna tsarin kiwon lafiya ya fara murmurewa da annobar Bayan shekaru uku na cutar COVID-19 tsarin kiwon lafiya a cikin ƙasashe sun fara nuna alamun farko na farfaɗowar tsarin kiwon lafiya a cewar rahoton wucin gadi na hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) game da zagaye na huɗu na binciken bugun jini na duniya kan cigaba da muhimman ayyukan kiwon lafiya a lokacin annobar COVID-19 Nuwaba 2022 January 2023. Ya zuwa farkon shekarar 2023, kasashe sun ba da rahoton samun raguwar cikas wajen isar da ayyukan kiwon lafiya na yau da kullun, amma sun nuna bukatar saka hannun jari don farfadowa da kuma karfin juriya na gaba. A cikin kasashe 139 da suka amsa zagaye na hudu na binciken bugun jini na hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO kasashe sun ba da rahoton ci gaba da wargajewa a kusan kashi daya bisa hudu na ayyuka a matsakaici. A cikin ƙasashe 84 inda za a iya nazarin yanayin, yawan ayyukan da aka katse ya ragu a matsaikata daga 56% a watan Yuli-Satumba 2020 zuwa 23% a watan Nuwamba 2022- Janairu 2023.
translations.hypothesis:

**Hausa Translation:**

Sabon sakamakon bincike sun nuna tsarin lafiya na farawa da dawowa kan hanya bayan jarrabawar cutar COVID-19. Bayan shekaru uku na jarrabawar cutar COVID-19, tsarin lafiya a kasashe daban-daban sun fara nuni da alama mafi yawan dawowar aikin aikin lafiya, kamar yadda rahoton da aka yi a matsayin wani rahotan da aka shirya a kan “Binciken duniya na karo na huɗɗa’in kan ci gaba da hidimomin lafiya na daidaito a lokacin jarrabawar cutar COVID-19: Nuwamba 2022 – Janairu 2023”.  A farkon shekarar 2023, kasashe sun bayyana cewa an rage tsangayawar samar da hidimomin lafiya na yau da kullun, amma sun jaddada buƙatar saka kuɗi don dawowa kan hanya da ƙarfi don gudanar da aiki a nan gaba.  A cikin kasashe 139 da suka amsa binciken WHO na karo na huɗɗa’in, kasashe sun bayyana cewa har yanzu akwai tsangayawar kusan kwata ɗaya na hidimomi. A kasashe 84 inda za’a yi aiyukan bincike, kashi na hidimomin da aka tsangaya ya ragu a matsakaita daga kashi 56% a watan Yuli – Satumba 2020 zuwa kashi 23% a tsakanin Nuwamba 2022 – Janairu 2023.

**Notes on Translation Choices & Considerations:**

*   **Formal Language:** I've used a relatively formal style of Hausa, appropriate for a report or news article.
*   **Technical Terms:** Terms like "health systems," "essential health services," and "pulse survey" are translated as closely as possible while maintaining clarity.  I've opted for direct translations where possible, as Hausa has a growing vocabulary to handle these concepts.
*   **Numbers:** Numbers are written in Arabic numerals, which is standard in Hausa writing.
*   **"COVID-19":**  I've kept "COVID-19" as is, as it's widely understood in Hausa.
*   **WHO:** I've kept WHO as is, as it's a well-known acronym.
*   **Sentence Structure:** I've adjusted sentence structure to flow naturally in Hausa, which sometimes requires rephrasing.
*   **"Trend Analysis":** Translated as "aiyukan bincike" which means "research activities" in this context.
*   **"Resilience":** Translated as "ƙarfi don gudanar da aiki" which means "strength to carry out work" to convey the meaning of resilience in this context.

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context and intended audience.

translations.source: Persisting disruptions are due to both demand- and supply-side factors, including low levels of health care-seeking in communities as well as limited availability of health workers and other health-care resources such as open clinics or available stocks of medicines and products. “It is welcome news that health systems in the majority of countries are starting to restore essential health services for millions of people who missed them during the pandemic,” said Dr Rudi Eggers, WHO Director for Integrated Health Services. “But we need to ensure that all countries continue to close this gap to recover health services, and apply lessons learnt to build more prepared and resilient health systems for the future”. First notable signs of recovery In this new survey, fewer countries reported intentionally scaling back access across all service delivery platforms and essential public health functions since 2020-2021 reporting, showing an important step to return to pre-pandemic levels of service delivery and broader system functioning.
translations.reference: Ci gaba da rikice-rikicen ya faru ne saboda abubuwan buƙatu- da abubuwan wadata, gami da ƙarancin matakan neman kiwon lafiya a cikin al'ummomi gami da ƙarancin wadatar ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya da sauran albarkatun kula da lafiya kamar buɗaɗɗen asibitoci ko samfuran magunguna da samfuran da ake samu. Abin maraba ne cewa tsarin kiwon lafiya a yawancin kasashe sun fara dawo da muhimman ayyukan kiwon lafiya ga miliyoyin mutanen da suka rasa su yayin barkewar cutar," in ji Dr Rudi Eggers, Daraktan Hukumar Kula da Lafiya ta Duniya. Amma muna buƙatar tabbatar da cewa dukkan ƙasashe suna ci gaba da rufe wannan rata don dawo da ayyukan kiwon lafiya, da kuma amfani da darussan da aka koya don gina shirye-shiryen kiwon lafiya da tsayayye don nan gaba". Alamun farko na samun lafiya A cikin wannan sabon binciken, ƙasashe kaɗan sun ba da rahoton ƙaddamar da damar samun dama ga duk dandamali na isar da sabis da mahimman ayyukan kiwon lafiyar jama'a tun daga rahoton 2020-2021, suna nuna muhimmin mataki na komawa zuw matakin kafin annoba da isar da sabis na da faffadan tsarin aiki.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided English document, aiming for clarity and accuracy. I've included some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Hausa Translation:**

Matsaloli da ke ci gaba da kasancewa suna faruwa ne sakamakon abubuwan da ke damƙar buƙatun (demand) da kuma samar da (supply) ayyukan lafiya, gami da ƙananan matakan neman aiki a bangaren lafiya a cikin al'umma, da kuma kawar da maƙasashiya da ke aiki a fannin lafiya da sauran abubuwan da ke ba da aikin lafiya, irin su asibitai da ake buɗewa ko kuma adadi mai yawa na magani da kayan aiki da ke nan.  “Murna ce cewa tsarin lafiya a mafi yawan kasashe ke da alƙawarin farfasa ayyukan lafiya na mahimmanci ga miliyoyin mutane da suka rasa su a lokacin jarrabawar cutar,” in ji Dr. Rudi Eggers, Daraktan Sabisai na Hadadden Lafiya a WHO. “Amma muna buƙatar tabbatar da cewa dukkan kasashe suna ci gaba da rufe wannan rashi don dawowa ayyukan lafiya, kuma a yi amfani da duk abubuwan da muka koya don gina tsarin lafiya da ya kasance shirye da kuma juriya don gaba.”

**Alama ta farko na canjawa zuwa alheri** A wannan sabon binciken, ƙanana kasashe suka bayyana cewa sun ƙara ƙara ƙididdigar canja canje a duk hanyoyin samar da aikin lafiya da kuma aikin lafiya na jama'a na gaba, wanda ke nuni da mataki mai mahimmanci don dawowa matakan aikin lafiya da suka kasance kafin jarrabawar, da kuma aikin tsarin gaba daya.

---

**Notes on Translation Choices & Considerations:**

*   **Technical Terms:** I've tried to use the most common and understandable Hausa terms for medical and health-related vocabulary.  Some terms are borrowed directly from English (e.g., "pandemic") as they are increasingly used in Hausa discourse, especially in formal settings.
*   **"Demand-side" and "Supply-side":** These are translated as "abubuwan da ke damƙar buƙatun" (things affecting demand) and "samar da" (supply) respectively. This is a common way to express these concepts in Hausa.
*   **"Health-care seeking":** Translated as "neman aiki a bangaren lafiya" (seeking work in the health sector).
*   **"Essential Health Services":** Translated as "ayyukan lafiya na mahimmanci" (important health services).
*   **"Integrated Health Services":** Translated as "Sabisai na Hadadden Lafiya" (Integrated Health Services).  This is a direct translation and is becoming more common.
*   **"Prepared and Resilient":** Translated as "shirye da kuma juriya" (prepared and resilient).
*   **"Scaling back access":** Translated as "ƙara ƙara ƙididdigar canja canje" (increasing the rate of change). This is a more natural way to express the concept in Hausa.
*   **Sentence Structure:** I've adjusted the sentence structure in some places to make it flow more naturally in Hausa.  Hausa often uses longer, more complex sentences than English.
*   **Formal Tone:** The translation maintains a formal tone appropriate for a document from the WHO.

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context and intended audience.
*   **Terminology Consistency:**  For ongoing projects, it's crucial to establish a glossary of terms to ensure consistency.

translations.source: By the end of 2022, most countries reported partial signs of service recovery, including in services for sexual, reproductive, maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health; nutrition; immunization; communicable diseases (including malaria, HIV, TB, and other sexually-transmitted infections); neglected tropical diseases; noncommunicable diseases; management of mental, neurological and substance use disorders; care for older people; and traditional and/or complementary care. The number of countries reporting disruption to their national supply chain system reduced from nearly half (29 of 59 responding countries) to about a quarter (18 of 66 responding countries) within the last year. Despite signs of recovery, service disruptions persist across countries in all regions and income levels, and across most service delivery settings and tracer service areas. Countries are also dealing with increasing service backlogs – most frequently in services for screening, diagnosis and treatment of noncommunicable diseases – which can lead to negative consequences as people are delayed access to timely care. Recovering essential health service delivery is critical because disruptions – including to services for health promotion, disease prevention, diagnosis, treatment, rehabilitation and palliation – may have even greater adverse health effects at population and individual level than the pandemic itself, especially among vulnerable populations.
translations.reference: A ƙarshen 2022, yawancin ƙasashe sun ba da rahoton alamun dawo da sabis na ɓangare, gami da sabis don jima'i, haihuwa, uwa, jarirai, yara da matasa; abinci mai gina jiki; rigakafi; cututtukan cututtuka (gami da zazzabin cizon sauro, HIV, tarin fuka, da sauran cututtukan da ake ɗauka ta hanyar jima'i); cuta mai zafi da aka manta da ita; cututtukan da ba a kamuwa da su; kula da rashin lafiyar taɓin ƙwaƙwalwa, jijiyoyin jiki da kuma amfani da kwayoyi; kula da tsofaffi; da kuma na gargajiya da / ko ƙarin kulawa. Adadin kasashen da ke bayar da rahoton cikas ga tsarin samar da kayayyaki na kasa ya ragu daga kusan rabin (29 daga cikin kasashe 59 da suka amsa) zuwa kusan kashi hudu (18 daga cikin kasashe 66 da suka amsa) a cikin shekarar da ta gabata. Duk da alamun farfadowa, hargitsin sabis na ci gaba da wanzuwa a cikin ƙasashe a duk yankuna da matakan samun kudin shiga, kuma a cikin mafi yawan saitunan isar da sabis da wuraren sabis na ganowa. Ƙasashe kuma suna fuskantar karuwar bayanan sabis - mafi yawan lokuta a cikin sabis don tantancewa, ganowa da kuma magance cututtukan da ba sa yaduwa - wanda zai haifar da mummunan sakamako yayin da mutane ke jinkirta samun kulawa akan lokaci. Farfado da mahimman isar da sabis na kiwon lafiya yana da mahimmanci saboda rikice-rikice - gami da sabis don haɓaka kiwon lafiya, rigakafin cututtuka, gwaje gwajd, jiyya, gyare-gyare - na iya haifar da mummunar illa ga lafiyar jama'a da kuma mutum fiye da annobar kanta, musamman ma. tsakanin jama'a masu rauni.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English text into Hausa, aiming for accuracy and clarity. I've included some notes after the translation to explain some choices and potential nuances.

**Hausa Translation:**

A ƙarshen shekarar 2022, mafi yawan kasashe sun bayar da rahoton alamu na wadatungiyar aikin aikin ilahuta, gami da ayyukan lafiya na jima'i, haihuwa, mahaifa, jarirai, yara da kuma matasa; abinci; rigakafin cuta; cutan da ke kaiwa cuta (gami da malaria, HIV, TB, da sauran cutan da ke yaduwa ta jima'i); cutan da ake rasa kulawa; cutan da ba sa yaduwa; kula da cutan tunani, jinin hawan jini da kuma amfani da kwayoyin narkewar abu; kula da manya; da kuma kula ta hanyar al'ada da/ko taimakon waje. Adadin kasashe da ke bayar da rahoton katasiryar tsarin samar da abinci a matakin kasa ya ragu daga kusan rabin (29 a cikin kasashe 59 da suka amsa) zuwa kusan kwatar (18 a cikin kasashe 66 da suka amsa) a cikin shekarar da ta wuce. Duk da alama da ke nuni da wadatungiya, katasiryar aikin ilahuta na ci gaba a kasashe daban-daban a duk fannin yankuna da matakan kudin ne, da kuma a cikin matsakaicin hanyar samar da aikin ilahuta da kuma fannin aikin ilahuta da ake biyo bayan sa. Kasashe ma suna fuskantar karuwar katasiryar aikin ilahuta – mafi yawan lokaci a ayyukan bincike, ganowa da kuma magance cutan da ba sa yaduwa – wanda hakan na iya haifar da sakamako mai illa yayin da mutane ke jinkunawa samun kulawar da ta dace a kan lokaci.  Wadatungiyar aikin ilahuta na da mahimmanci saboda katasiriyar – gami da ayyukan karfafa lafiya, hana cuta, ganowa, magani, gyara hali da kuma kawar da cuta – na iya zama da mummunan tasiri akan lafiyar jama'a da kuma lafiyar mutane, fiye da cutar kanta, musamman a cikin wadanda ke cikin halin rauni.

**Notes on Translation Choices & Potential Nuances:**

*   **"Service Recovery"**: I translated this as "wadatungiyar aikin ilahuta" (improvement/restoration of health services).  "Wadatungiya" is a common term for recovery or improvement in Hausa.
*   **"Sexual, Reproductive, Maternal, Newborn, Child and Adolescent Health"**: This is a long phrase. I tried to keep it as concise as possible while still being accurate.
*   **"Neglected Tropical Diseases"**: Translated as "cutan da ake rasa kulawa" (diseases that are neglected/lacking care).
*   **"Noncommunicable Diseases"**: Translated as "cutan da ba sa yaduwa" (diseases that don't spread).
*   **"Mental, Neurological and Substance Use Disorders"**: This was a tricky one. I opted for "kula da cutan tunani, jinin hawan jini da kuma amfani da kwayoyin narkewar abu" (care for mental illnesses, high blood pressure, and drug use).
*   **"Supply Chain System"**: Translated as "tsarin samar da abinci" (supply system).
*   **"Tracer Service Areas"**: This is a technical term. I translated it as "fannin aikin ilahuta da ake biyo bayan sa" (areas of health work that are being tracked/followed).
*   **"Service Backlogs"**: Translated as "katasiryar aikin ilahuta" (disruptions/backlog of health services).
*   **"Palliation"**: Translated as "kawar da cuta" (relieving/alleviating illness).
*   **"Vulnerable Populations"**: Translated as "wadanda ke cikin halin rauni" (those in a vulnerable situation).

**Important Considerations:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context. If this document is for a very technical audience, some of the phrasing might need to be adjusted.
*   **Hausa Dialects:** Hausa has different dialects. I've aimed for a fairly standard, widely understood form.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:** It's *highly recommended* that a native Hausa speaker, preferably someone with experience in health or development communication, review this translation for accuracy and clarity.

translations.source: Integrating COVID-19 services into essential health services In another important step towards system recovery and transition, most countries have made progress in integrating COVID-19 services into routine health service delivery. About 80-90% of countries have fully integrated COVID-19 vaccination, diagnostic and case management services as well as services for post COVID-19 condition into routine service delivery. Still, most countries (80% of 83 responding countries) reported at least one bottleneck to scaling up access to essential COVID-19 tools (e.g. COVID-19 diagnostics, therapeutics, vaccines and personal protective equipment - PPE), with health workforce issues and lack of funding representing the most common barriers. Further support needed for recovery, resilience and preparedness Most countries have started to apply what they have learnt during the COVID-19 pandemic, including through the institutionalization of a number of innovative service disruption mitigation strategies into routine health service delivery.
translations.reference: Haɗa ayyukan COVID-19 a cikin mahimman ayyukan kiwon lafiya A wani muhimmin mataki na dawo da tsarin da sauyawa, yawancin ƙasashe sun sami ci gaba wajen haɗa ayyukan COVID-19 a cikin isar da sabis na kiwon lafiya na yau da kullum. Kimanin kashi 80-90% na ƙasashe sun haɗa allurar rigakafin COVID-19, bincike da sabis na kula da lamura da kuma sabis don yanayin bayan COVID-19 cikin isar da sabis na yau da kullum. Har yanzu, yawancin ƙasashe (80% na 83 na ƙasashe masu ba da amsa) sun ba da rahoton aƙalla ƙugiya ɗaya don haɓaka damar yin amfani da mahimman kayan aikin COVID-19 (misali COVID-19 bincike, warkewa, alluran rigakafi da kayan kariya na sirri - PPE), tare da batutuwan ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya da rashin kuɗi da ke wakiltar mafi yawan shinge. Ƙarin tallafi da ake buƙata don farfadowa, juriya da shirye-shirye Yawancin ƙasashe sun fara amfani da abin da suka koya yayin annobar COVID-19, gami da aiwatar da wasu sabbin dabarun rage rikicewar sabis a cikin isar da sabis na kiwon lafiya na yau da kullum.

Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided document, aiming for clarity and accuracy. I've included some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Haussa Translation:**

Haɗa ayyukan COVID-19 da ayyukan kiwon lafiya na dawo daidaito. A mataki na wani muhimanci don farfafa da tsarin lafiya da canzawa, kasashe da dama sun yi ci gaba da haɗa ayyukan COVID-19 da aikin yau da kullun na kiwon lafiya. Kusan kasashe 80-90 sun haɗa cikakke ayyukan alluran rigakafin COVID-19, binciken cuta da kula da marasa lafiya, da kuma ayyukan kula da mutanen da suka kamu da cutar COVID-19 bayan kamuwa da cuta a cikin aikin yau da kullun. Duk da haka, kasashe da yawa (80% na kasashe 83 da suka amsa) sun bayyana aƙalla wata matsala a kan ƙara yawan samun kayan aikin da suka dace don COVID-19 (alalace, binciken cuta, magani, alluran rigakafi da kayan kariya - PPE), tare da matsalar ma'aikatan lafiya da rashin kudin da ya fi kowanne wani cikas.

Ana bukatar karin goyon baya don farfafa, jaddada da shirye da shirye. Kasashe da dama sun fara amfanawa da abubuwan da suka koya yayin jarrabawar COVID-19, gami da shigar da wasu hanyoyin magance rushewar aikin kiwon lafiya a cikin aikin yau da kullun.

**Notes on Translation Choices & Considerations:**

*   **Terminology:** I've used common Hausa terms for COVID-19 related concepts.  "Alluran rigakafi" is the standard term for "vaccine." "Binciken cuta" is for "diagnostic testing." "PPE" is often left as is, or explained as "kayan kariya" (protective equipment).
*   **Sentence Structure:** I've adjusted sentence structure to flow naturally in Hausa.  Direct, word-for-word translations often sound awkward.
*   **"Bottleneck":**  I translated "bottleneck" as "cikas" which means obstacle or hindrance.
*   **"Institutionalization":** I used "shigar da" which means to incorporate or integrate.
*   **Clarity:** I aimed for clear and understandable language, avoiding overly technical terms where possible.
*   **Numbers:** Numbers are written as they are commonly used in Hausa.
*   **Responding Countries:** I used "kasashe da suka amsa" which means countries that responded.

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  The best translation always depends on the specific context and intended audience.
*   **Review by a Native Speaker:**  It's *crucial* to have a native Hausa speaker review this translation for accuracy, naturalness, and cultural appropriateness.  I am an AI and cannot guarantee perfect accuracy.

translations.source: These include deployment of telemedicine approaches, promotion of home-based care or self-care interventions, approaches for strengthening health workers availability, capacities and support mechanisms, innovations in procuring and delivering medicines and supplies, more routine community communications, and partnerships with private sector providers. Three quarters of countries reported additional funding allocation towards longer term system recovery, resilience and preparedness. Countries expressed need for WHO support to address remaining challenges in the COVID-19 context and beyond, most frequently related to health workforce strengthening, building the monitoring capacities of health services, designing primary health care -- oriented models of care, governance, policy and planning and financial planning and funding. Note to editors: In the fourth round of WHO’s global pulse survey, 222 countries, territories and areas were invited to respond to a standardized web-based survey between November 2022 and January 2023. The fourth survey followed up on WHO’s previous 2020 and 2021 pulse surveys: Round 1 (May-September 2020), Round 2 (January-March 2021), and Round 3 (November-December 2021) which showed the extent to which the pandemic was affecting the continuity of essential health services and how countries are taking action.
translations.reference: Waɗannan sun haɗa da ƙaddamar da hanyoyin sadarwar telemedicine, haɓaka kulawar gida ko ayyukan kulawa da kai, hanyoyin ƙarfafa wadatar ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya, iyawa da hanyoyin tallafi, sabbin abubuwa a cikin samarwa da isar da magunguna da kayayyaki, ƙarin hanyoyin sadarwa na yau da kullun, da haɗin gwiwa tare da kamfanoni masu zaman kansu. masu bayarwa. Kashi uku cikin huɗu na ƙasashe sun ba da rahoton ƙarin tallafin kuɗi don dawo da tsarin dogon lokaci, juriya da shirye shirye. sanarwa ga editoci: A zagaye na hudu na binciken bugun jini na na hukumar lafiya ta duniyaWHO na duniya, an gayyaci kasashe, yankuna da yankuna 222 don amsa daidaitaccen binciken yanar gizo tsakanin Nuwamba 2022 da Janairu 2023. Sanarwa ga editoci: A zagaye na hudu na binciken bugun jini na na hukumar lafiya ta duniyaWHO na duniya, an gayyaci kasashe, yankuna da yankuna 222 don amsa daidaitaccen binciken yanar gizo tsakanin Nuwamba 2022 da Janairu 2023. Binciken na hudu ya biyo bayan binciken hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO na baya na 2020 da 2021: Zagaye na 1 (Mayu-Satumba 2020), Zagaye na 2 (Janairu-Maris 2021), da Zagaye na 3 (Nuwamba-Disamba 2021) wanda ya nuna girman annobar ta shafi ci gaba da muhimman ayyukan kiwon lafiya da kuma yadda kasashe ke daukar matakai.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's the Hausa translation of the provided English document. I've aimed for accuracy and clarity, keeping in mind the context of health and international organizations. I've also included some notes after the translation explaining some choices I made.

**Hausa Translation:**

Waɗannan sun haɗa da aiwatar da hanyoyin telemedicine, haɓɓaka da kula da cuta a gida ko tsaurara ayyukan jinyewa da mutane suke yi, hanyoyin ƙirƙirar ƙarfin ma'aikatan lafiya, ƙwarewarsu da tsarin tallafi, sabbin hanyoyin sayan magani da abubuwan da ake buƙata, samar da bayanin hankali da yawa ga al'umma, da haɗin gwanon kai da masu samar da hidimomi na dangi. Kusan rabi na ƙasashe sun bayyana cewa an ƙara kuɗi don tallafawa tsarin lafiya na dogon lokaci, jujjuyawa da shirye-shiryen da za a biyo baya. Kasashe sun nuna buƙatar taimakon WHO don warware matsalolin da suka rage a yanayin COVID-19 da bayan sa, galibi dangane da ƙirƙirar ƙarfin ma'aikatan lafiya, ƙirƙirar ƙarfin bincike na ayyukan lafiya, ƙirar samfurin kula da lafiya wanda ya mai da hankali kan kula da lafiya na farko, mulki, manufa da shiri da shiri da kudi. Bayanai ga edita: A zagawar huɗu ta binciken duniya na WHO, an aiko da kusan ƙasashe, yankuna da sassan 222 da su amsa ga binciken da aka kafa a kan intanet tsakanin Nuwamba 2022 da Janairu 2023. Binciken na huɗu ya biyo bayan binciken da WHO ya yi a shekarun 2020 da 2021: Round 1 (Mayu-Satumba 2020), Round 2 (Janairu-Marci 2021), da Round 3 (Nuwamba-Disamba 2021) wanda ya nuna yadda jarrabawar cutar ta shafar ci gaba da hidimomin lafiya da kuma yadda kasashe ke ɗaukar mataki.

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **Telemedicine:** I used "aiwatar da hanyoyin telemedicine" which translates to "implementing telemedicine approaches." This is a common way to refer to it in Hausa.
*   **Home-based care/Self-care:** I used "kula da cuta a gida ko tsaurara ayyukan jinyewa da mutane suke yi" which means "caring for illness at home or intensifying self-care activities."
*   **Health workers availability, capacities and support mechanisms:** I translated this as "ƙirƙirar ƙarfin ma'aikatan lafiya, ƙwarewarsu da tsarin tallafi" - "building the strength of health workers, their expertise and support systems."
*   **Procuring and delivering medicines and supplies:** "sabin hanyoyin sayan magani da abubuwan da ake buƙata" - "new ways of buying medicine and needed items."
*   **Community communications:** "samar da bayanin hankali da yawa ga al'umma" - "providing a lot of important information to the community."
*   **Private sector providers:** "masu samar da hidimomi na dangi" - "family service providers."

translations.source: While pulse surveys have limitations such as reporting bias and representativeness, the strength of this effort is that it is comprehensive and delivers information rapidly. The term “country” should be understood to include all countries, territories and areas. Trend analyses was completed based on responses from 84 countries, territories, or areas that responded to at least one survey section in all four pulse survey rounds.
translations.reference: Yayin da binciken bugun jini yake da iyakoki kamar bayar da rahoto na ɓangaranci da wakilci, ƙarfin wannan ƙoƙarin shine yana da cikakke kuma yana ba da bayanai cikin sauri. Yakamata a fahimci kalmar "ƙasa" ta ƙunshi dukkan kasashe, yankuna da wurare. An kammala nazarin abubuwan da ke faruwa bisa la'akari da martani daga ƙasashe 84, yankuna, ko wurare da suka amsa aƙalla sashin bincike ɗaya a cikin duk zagayen binciken bugun jini guda huɗu.

translations.source: Statement of celebration for HRP’s 50th anniversary: Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland To capture the spirit of Policy and Coordination Committee (PCC) members celebrating HRP’s 50th anniversary and its numerous achievements, Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland read the following note of appreciation and congratulations at the 36th meeting of the HRP PCC. Our generation has seen more progress in science and technology than all preceding generations together and witnessed several intellectual, technological, medical and social transformation spearheaded by the contraceptive revolution and followed by advances in reproductive health, health innovations as well as rights and gender equality. However, considerable challenges in the field of SRHR continue, and, arguably, some advances are in question again today. We applaud HRP-staff, partners and supporters on the numerous achievements to date and recognise the enormous strides accomplished during half a century in producing the evidence that supports full and equal access to sexual and reproductive health and rights.
translations.reference: Sanarwa na bikin cika shekaru 50 na HRP: Sweden da daular Ingila ta Birtaniya arewacin Ireland Don ɗaukar siyasa mai rai membobin Kwamitin Manufofi da Gudanarwa (PCC) waɗanda ke bikin cika shekaru 50 na HRP da nasarorin da aka samu, Sweden da daular Ingila ta Birtaniya da arewacin Ireland karanta wannan bayanin godiya da taya murna a taron na 36th na HRP. Farashin PCC. Zamaninmu ya sami ci gaba a fannin kimiyya da fasaha fiye da sauran al'ummomin da suka gabata tare kuma sun shaidi sauye-sauye da dama na ilimi, fasaha, likitanci da zamantakewa wanda juyin juya halin hana daukar ciki ya jagoranta sannan kuma ya biyo bayan ci gaban kiwon lafiyar haihuwa, sabbin hanyoyin kiwon lafiya gami da hakkoki da daidaiton jinsi. Kodayake, manyan ƙalubale a fagen SRHR suna ci gaba, kuma, a zahiri, ana sake tambayar wasu ci gaba a yau. Muna yaba wa ma'aikatan HRP-, abokan tarayya da masu goyan baya kan nasarorin da aka samu har zuwa yau kuma mun ga babban ci gaba da aka cimma a cikin rabin karni na samar da shaidun da ke goyan bayan cikakkiyar dama ga lafiyar jima'i da haihuwa da hakki.
translations.hypothesis:

Sanarwa da nuni da jinjiri da sababbin kwarewar HRP da kwanaki 50: Sweden da United Kingdom of Great Britain da Northern Ireland

Don kama rawar bambanar mambobin kwamitin Tsari da Haɗin gwiwa (PCC) da ke jinjingar da kwanaki 50 na HRP da nasarar da ta samu, Sweden da United Kingdom of Great Britain da Northern Ireland sun karanta wannan sanarwar godiya da taya jinjina a taron PCC na 36.

Karni na yau ya ga ci gaban ilimi da fasaha fiye da duk manyan karni da suka gabata, kuma ya ga canje-canje na ilimi, fasaha, likita da zamantawa, wanda ya fara ne da juyin juyaicin hana haihuwa, sannan ya biyo bayan ci gaban lafiya mai laushi, sabbin hanyoyin lafiya, da kuma haƙƙin mutum da jimaƙi.  Koyashi, har yanzu akwai matsaloli da yawa a fannin lafiya mai laushi da jimaƙi, kuma, a zahiri, wasu ci gaba na iya zubar da kasala a yau.

Muna yabawa ma'aikata, abokan tarayya da masu tallafi na HRP akan nasarar da ta samu har zuwa yau, kuma muna godon matakin babban ci gaba da aka samu a cikin kwanaki 50 a samar da bincike da ke tallafawa shiga cikin cikakken lafiya da haƙƙin jimaƙi.

translations.source: We would like to outline three major categories of achievements: Firstly, the high-quality research by HRP has had tangible impact on public health policy, standards, and practice worldwide addressing the evolving needs of diverse populations. Secondly, HRP, notably through the HRP Alliance, has strengthened research institutions locally and globally, and, enabled numerous researchers to acquire the research skills that ensure that high-quality SRHR research can continue, and indeed increase, into the future. Thirdly, in a world beset by crisis, conflict and increasing resistance to gender equality, HRP has facilitated the upholding of the basic rights of women and girls and vulnerable populations. HRP remains the main instrument within the United Nations system for research in human reproduction and continues to be an exceptional leader in global sexual and reproductive health.
translations.reference: Muna so mu fayyace manyan nau'ikan nasarori guda uku: Da fari dai, babban bincike na HRP ya yi tasiri mai ma'ana kan manufofin kiwon lafiyar jama'a, ka'idoji, da ayyuka a duk duniya don magance buƙatun ci gaba na al'umma daban-daban. Na biyu, HRP, musamman ta hanyar HRP Alliance, ya ƙarfafa cibiyoyin bincike a cikin gida da kuma na duniya, kuma, ya ba wa masu bincike da yawa damar samun ƙwarewar bincike wanda ke tabbatar da cewa bincike mai inganci na SRHR zai iya ci gaba, kuma ya haɓaka, a nan gaba. Na uku, a cikin duniyar da ke fama da tashin hankali, rikice-rikice da ƙaruwar adawa ga daidaiton jinsi, HRP ta sauƙaƙe kiyaye haƙƙin mata da 'yan mata da kuma al'ummomi masu rauni. HRP ya kasance babban kayan aiki a cikin tsarin Majalisar Dinkin Duniya don bincike game da haifuwar ɗan adam kuma ci gaba da kasancewa jagora musamman a lafiyar jima'i da haihuwa ta duniya.

Na biyu, HRP, musamman ta HRP Alliance, ta ƙarfafa ƙungiyoyin bincike a cikin gida da na duniya, kuma ta ba da damar bincikun da dama don samun ƙwarewar bincike da ke tabbatar da cewa binciken SRHR mai inganci na iya ci gaba, kuma ya ƙaru, a gaba. Na uku, a duniya da ke fuskantar rikici, kasala, da adawa da juyin halitta, HRP ta ba da taimako wajen kiyaye haƙƙin asali na mata da 'yan mata da kuma al'ummomin da ke cikin halin rauni. translations.source: HRP and its partners have brought us closer to the promise of the 1994 International Conference on Population and Development and its call to put the rights of individuals, especially women and girls, at the heart of sustainable development. We want to express our appreciation and gratitude to the HRP secretariat, its partners, co-sponsors and the global network of collaborating centres and experts helping to ensure its efforts contribute to making a difference to the women, men, children, adolescents and various communities HRP is committed to serve. On behalf of PCC members, allow us to reaffirm our partnership with you to support the achievements of HRP, upon which the success of the 2030 Agenda depends.
translations.reference: HRP da takwarorinta sun kusantar da mu ga alkawarin da aka yi na taron kasa da kasa kan yawan jama’a da ci gaba a shekarar 1994 da kuma kiran da ta yi na sanya hakkin daidaikun mutane, musamman mata da ‘yan mata a kan tushen cigaban muradan ƙarni. Muna so mu bayyana godiya da jinjina ga sakatariyar HRP, abokan hulɗarta, masu ba da tallafi da cibiyoyin haɗin gwiwar duniya da masana da ke taimakawa wajen tabbatar da kokarinta na taimakawa wajen kawo canji ga mata, maza, yara, matasa da kuma al'ummomi daban-daban. HRP ta himmatu don yin hidima. A madadin membobin PCC, ta bamu damar sake tabbatar da haɗin gwiwa tare da ku don tallafawa nasarorin HRP, wanda nasarar 2030 Agenda ya dogara.
translations.hypothesis:

Muna son bayyana babban kaunata da godiya ga ofishin HRP, abokan gabansa, abokan taimakon, da kuma cibiyoyin hadin gwanon duniya da masana da ke taimakon tabbatar da kokawar su ta kawo canjin rayuwar mata, maza, yara, matasa, da kuma al'ummomi daban-daban da HRP ta himmatu kan yi wa hidima. translations.source: WHO launches new initiative to improve pandemic preparedness To help countries better prepare for future pandemics, WHO launched a new initiative today that provides guidance on integrated planning for responding to any respiratory pathogen such as influenza or coronaviruses. The new Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats Initiative, or PRET, incorporates the latest tools and approaches for shared learning and collective action established during the COVID-19 pandemic and other recent public health emergencies. Through the initiative, WHO will use a mode of transmission approach to guide countries in pandemic planning, given that many capacities and capabilities are common among groups of pathogens. PRET answers the call for technical guidance and support for promoting and strengthening integrated preparedness and response, as outlined in World Health Assembly resolutions.
translations.reference: Hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO ta ƙaddamar da wani sabon shiri don inganta shirye-shiryen annoba Don taimaka wa ƙasashe su shirya don faruwar annoba a gaba,hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO ta ƙaddamar da wani sabon shiri a yau wanda ke ba da jagora kan haɗaɗɗun tsare-tsare don ba da amsa ga duk wani cututtukan numfashi kamar mura ko coronaviruses. Sabon Tsari da Juriya don Ƙaddamar da wani tsari, ko PRET, ta haɗa sabbin kayan aiki da hanyoyin ilmantarwa da ayyukan gama kai da aka kafa yayin bala'in COVID-19 da sauran abubuwan gaggawa na lafiyar jama'a na baya-bayan nan. Ta hanyar shirin hukumar lafiya ta duniya, WHO za ta yi amfani da tsarin watsa shirye-shirye don jagorantar kasashe a cikin shirye-shiryen annoba, ganin cewa da dama sun zama ruwan dare tsakanin kungiyoyin masu cutar. PRET tana amsa kiran jagorar fasaha da goyan baya don haɓaka da ƙarfafa shirye-shirye a haɗe da amsa, kamar yadda aka tsara a cikin kudurori na Majalisar Lafiya ta Duniya.
translations.hypothesis:

translations.source: The COVID-19 pandemic and other health emergencies have shown that countries need to be operationally ready to respond to infectious disease threats, with tailored preparedness plans in hand and better coordination and collaboration with other sectors such as agriculture. “Preparedness, prevention, and response activities must not be the province of the health sector alone,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Just as health emergencies have impacts across many sectors, so must our preparedness and response efforts span sectors, disciplines and pathogens. It is critical, too, that community engagement and equity are the centre of our efforts, especially for those populations that are marginalized and most at risk.” PRET is an evolution in WHO’s approach to pandemic preparedness through the application of a mode of transmission lens, rather than a focus on specific diseases.
translations.reference: Cutar ta COVID-19 da sauran matsalolin gaggawa na kiwon lafiya sun nuna cewa akwai bukatar kasashe su kasance cikin shiri don magance barazanar kamuwa da cuta, tare da tsare-tsaren shirye-shiryen da aka kera a hannu da ingantacciyar daidaituwa da hadin gwiwa da sauran bangarorin kamar aikin gona. Tsarin shirye-shirye, rigakafi, da mayar da martani ba dole ba ne su kasance lardin da ke bangaren lafiya kaɗai bai,” in ji Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Darakta Janar na hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO. Kamar yadda matsalolin gaggawa na kiwon lafiya ke yin tasiri a sassa da dama, haka nan dole ne a shirin mu da kokarin mayar da martani ga ɓangarori, darussa da cututtuka. Yana da mahimmanci kuma, cewa haɗin kai da daidaito tsakanin al'umma su ne cibiyar ƙoƙarinmu, musamman ga waɗanda ba a sani ba kuma galibi suna cikin haɗari. PRET juyin halitta ne a tsarin WHO game da shirye-shiryen kamuwa da cutar ta hanyar aikace-aikacen yanayin watsa ruwan tabarau, maimakon mayar da hankali kan takamaiman cututtuka.

"Kamar yadda harkokin lafiya ke da tasiri a fannin bangarori da yawa, haka ma ayyukan shirye-shirye da amsawa na yakamata su wuce bangarori, fannin ilimi da cutacce. Hakanan yana da mahimmanci, cewa amsa da kuma daidaito yakamata su zama a tsakiyar ayyukanmu, musamman ga wadanda suka barin al'umma da kuma wadanda ke cikin haddasa barazana mafi yawa." translations.source: WHO will continue to develop and disseminate guidance on specific diseases as needed. The launch of the initiative is taking place on the last day of meetings with over 120 individuals representing Member States, partners, and the WHO secretariat, to define the path forward for PRET, including the development of a global implementation roadmap for respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. The PRET Initiative’s first module focuses on respiratory pathogens, including influenza, coronaviruses, and respiratory syncytial virus. Given the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic and the possible threat of avian influenza, this module will enable countries to critically review, test, and update their respiratory pandemic planning efforts to ensure they have the functional capacities and capabilities in place. A process is underway to identify the next group of pathogens, such as arboviruses, to be addressed under this initiative.
translations.reference: WHO za ta ci gaba da haɓakawa da yada jagora kan takamaiman cututtuka kamar yadda ake buƙata. Kaddamar da shirin yana gudana ne a rana ta ƙarshe ta tarurruka tare da mutane sama da 120 da ke wakiltar ƙasashe membobin, abokan tarayya, da sakatariyar WHO, don ayyana hanyar ci gaba don PRET, gami da haɓaka taswirar aiwatarwa ta duniya don shirye-shiryen rigakafin cututtukan ƙwayar cuta na numfashi. Tsarin farko na PRET Initiative yana mai da hankali kan ƙwayoyin cuta na numfashi, gami da mura, coronaviruses, da ƙwayar cuta ta syncytial na numfashi. Ganin yadda annobar cutar ta COVID-19 take cigaba da kuma yuwuwar barazanar kamuwa da mura ta jiragen ruwa, wannan tsarin zai baiwa kasashe damar yin nazari sosai, gwadawa, da sabunta yunƙurin shirye-shiryen cutar sankarau don tabbatar da cewa suna da iya aiki da iya aiki a wurin. Ana ci gaba da aiki don gano rukuni na gaba na ƙwayoyin cuta, irin su arboviruses, da za a magance a ƙarƙashin wannan shirin.

translations.source: This will follow priorities identified through the ten proposals to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience (HEPR). The PRET Initiative ushers in a new era for pandemic preparedness and represents an evolution of WHO’s core activities to support all Member States in strengthening health emergency preparedness, prevention, and response capacities and capabilities. The initiative can also serve to operationalize the objectives and provisions of the Pandemic Accord, which is currently being negotiated by WHO Member States. Following inputs and discussions among meeting participants, WHO invites stakeholders to this global call to action to advance respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. Call to Action
translations.reference: Wannan zai bi manyan abubuwan da aka gano ta hanyar shawarwari goma don ƙarfafa gine-ginen duniya don shirye-shiryen gaggawa na lafiya, amsawa, da juriya (HEPR). Ƙaddamar da PRET ta haifar da sabon zamani don shirye-shiryen cutar sankara kuma tana wakiltar juyin halitta na ainihin ayyukan hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO don tallafawa duk Membobin Kasashe don ƙarfafa shirye-shiryen gaggawa na kiwon lafiya, rigakafi, da damar amsawa da iyawa. Har ila yau, shirin na iya yin aiki don aiwatar da manufofi da tanade-tanade na Yarjejeniyar Cutar Kwalara, wadda a halin yanzu ƙasashe membobin hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO ke tattaunawa. Bayan bayanai da tattaunawa tsakanin mahalarta taron, hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO ta gayyaci masu ruwa da tsaki zuwa wannan kiran na duniya don aiwatar da shirye-shiryen rigakafin cutar sankarau. Kira zuwa ga Aiki

translations.source: WHO, Member States, and partners are working together to identify and implement priority actions to strengthen national, regional, and global preparedness for pandemics and emerging infectious disease threats. We recognize the role of communities and all sectors, and the need for harmonized and sustained momentum to end the cycle of panic and neglect that is all too common in pandemic preparedness and response. We recall lessons from previous epidemics and pandemics, especially those caused by respiratory pathogens. These pathogens have and will continue to pose a significant global threat with the potential to cause tremendous morbidity and mortality, overwhelm health systems, de-stabilize the global economy, and exacerbate inequities, which exist in the access to both the tools to prevent pandemics and health care for all people. This is a Call to Action to accelerate preparedness for pandemics and emerging threats globally.
translations.reference: Hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO, Membobin Kasashe, da abokan haɗin gwiwa suna aiki tare don ganowa da aiwatar da ayyuka masu fifiko don ƙarfafa shirye-shiryen ƙasa, yanki, da duniya don cututtukan cututtuka da kuma barazanar kamuwa da cuta. Mun fahimci rawar al'ummomi da dukkan sassa, da buƙatar daidaitawa da dorewar ci gaba don kawo ƙarshen firgici da sakaci wanda ya zama ruwan dare a cikin shirye-shiryen annoba da martani. Mun tuna da darussa daga bala'i da annoba da suka gabata, musamman wadanda cututtukan numfashi ke haifar da su. Wadannan cututtukan suna kuma za su ci gaba da haifar da babbar barazana ta duniya tare da yuwuwar haifar da mummunar cututtuka da mace-mace, mamaye tsarin kiwon lafiya, dagula tattalin arzikin duniya, da kuma ta'azzara rashin daidaito, wanda ke wanzuwa a cikin damar yin amfani da kayan aikin biyu don hana kamuwa da cuta kiwon lafiya ga dukan mutane. Wannan Kira ne zuwa Aiki don haɓaka shirye-shiryen annoba da barazanar da ke fitowa a duniya.
translations.hypothesis:
Okay, here's a translation of the provided English text into Hausa, aiming for clarity and accuracy. I've included some notes afterward explaining some of the choices made.

**Hausa Translation:**

Kungiyar WHO, Kasashen Memba, da abokan aikawa suna jaje jaje don gano da aiwatar da ayyukan da suka fi mahimmanci don karfafa shirye-shiryen da ke kan gaba a matakin kasa, yankewa, da duniya don yakar cutan kwayayyar halita da ke bayyana. Muna godon rawar al'umma da dukkan bangarorin, da kuma buƙatar ƙarƙashin da kuma ci gaba da ƙarfin gwiwa don kawo ƙarshen juyin hankali da rashin kulawa da ake gani a shirye-shiryen da amsar cutar gurguci.  Muna tunawa da darussan da muka samu daga cutukan gurguci da suka gabata da kuma izuwa yanzu, musamman wadanda ke faruwa ne ta hanyar cutar da ke shafar iska. Waɗannan cutukan sun kasance da kuma zasu ci gaba da kasancewa barazana mai ƙarfi a duniya, da kuma yiwuwar haifar da cutewa da mutuwar yawa, mamɓance tsarin lafiya, yaudarar tattalin arziƙin duniya, da ƙara tsanwa ga bambancin da ke nan a kan samun kayan hana cutar gurguci da kuma kula da lafiya ga dukkan mutane. Wannan shi ne kira don aiwatar da ayyuka don saurin shirye-shiryen da ke kan gaba don yaki da cutar gurguci da barazanar da ke bayyana a duniya.

**Pronunciation Guide (approximate - Hausa pronunciation can be nuanced):**

*   **Kungiyar WHO:**  Kung-yah-yar WHO
*   **Kasashen Memba:**  Kah-sah-shen Mem-bah

*   **darussan da muka samu:** Dah-roo-ssahn dah moo-kah sah-moo
*   **cutukan gurguci:** Coo-tsoo-kahn goor-goo-chee
*   **musamman wadanda ke faruwa ne ta hanyar cutar da ke shafar iska:** Moo-sah-mahn wah-dahn-dah keh far-oo-wah neh tah hahn-yah coo-tahr dah keh shah-far ees-kah
*   **barazana mai ƙarfi:** Bah-rah-zah-nah mai kahr-fee
*   **cutewa da mutuwar yawa:** Coo-teh-wah dah moo-too-war yah-wah
*   **mamɓance tsarin lafiya:** Mahm-bahn-seh tsaa-reen lah-fee-yah
*   **yaudarar tattalin arziƙin duniya:** Yah-doh-rah-tahr tat-tah-leen ar-zee-keen doon-yah

*   **kayan hana cutar gurguci:** Kah-yaan hah-nah coo-tahr goor-goo-chee
*   **kula da lafiya ga dukkan mutane:** Koo-lah dah lah-fee-yah gah dook-kahn moo-tah-neh
*   **kiran don aiwatar da ayyuka:** Kee-rah dohn eye-wah-tah-tah dah eye-yoo-kah
*   **saurin shirye-shiryen da ke kan gaba:** Sah-oo-reen shee-reh-shee-ren dah keh kahn gah-bah
*   **yaki da cutar gurguci da barazanar da ke bayyana a duniya:** Yah-kee dah coo-tahr goor-goo-chee dah bah-rah-zah-nah dah keh bah-yah-nah ah doon-yah

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

*   **"Working together" (jaje jaje):** This is an idiomatic expression in Hausa to convey the idea of collaborative effort.  A more literal translation would be awkward.
*   **"Pandemic preparedness and response":** I used "shirye-shiryen da ke kan gaba da amsar cutar gurguci" which translates to "preparation for the future and response to the pandemic."
*   **"Respiratory pathogens":**  "cutar da ke shafar iska" - literally "disease that affects the air." This is a common way to refer to respiratory illnesses.
*   **"Morbidity and mortality":**  "cutewa da mutuwar yawa" - "sickness and many deaths."
*   **"Call to Action":** "kiran don aiwatar da ayyuka" - "a call to implement actions."
*   **Hausa word order:** I've tried to maintain a natural Hausa sentence structure, which can differ from English.
*   **Formal vs. Informal:** This translation leans towards a more formal register, suitable for an official document.

translations.source: Effective preparedness relies on robust planning and coordinated action. A collective commitment on the following actions will see progress achieved by December 2025 as will be described in the (Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET) monitoring framework: Update preparedness plans that affirm priority actions and that have considered learnings from past events. Recognizing the risk posed by respiratory pathogens, planning for a respiratory pathogen pandemic based on the themes identified in the PRET Module #1: Planning for Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics is a priority
translations.reference: Shiri mai inganci ya dogara ne akan ingantaccen tsari da aiki tare. Alƙawarin gamayya kan ayyuka masu zuwa za su kai ga ci gaban da aka samu nan da Disamba 2025 kamar yadda za a bayyana a cikin tsarin sa ido na (Shiri da Juriya don Barazana masu tasowa (PRET): Sabunta shirye-shiryen da ke tabbatar da ayyukan fifiko kuma waɗanda suka yi la'akari da koyo daga abubuwan da suka faru a baya. Gane haɗarin da ƙwayoyin cuta na numfashi ke haifarwa, tsarawa don da annobar cutar numfashi dangane da jigogin da aka gano a cikin Module PRET #1: Abu ne mai muhimmanci zama cikin shirin fuskantar annobar cututtuka masu alaƙa da numfashi.

translations.source: Increase connectivity among stakeholders in pandemic preparedness planning through systematic coordination and cooperation. This includes building equitable systems; conducting joint exercises; and sharing information on good practices, challenges, and opportunities. Dedicate sustained investments, financing and monitoring of pandemic preparedness with a particular focus on addressing the gaps identified during past pandemics and epidemics. The COVID-19 pandemic response has demonstrated what can be achieved with political commitment, community engagement and funding.

Ware kuɗi na musamman, ɗaukar nauyi da shirya bibiyar tasirin annobar da kuma ɗaukar mataki ta la'akari da giɓin da aka samu a yayin wata annoba a baya. matakin da aka ɗauka kan Kwabid-19 'yar manuniya ce game irin nasarar da za a samu daga shugabanci da al'umma da tallafi.

translations.source: At the heart of this work is to ensure equity to be ready for the next pandemic together. Whole-of-society action is needed to make the progress outlined in this Call to Action. Implementation should therefore strengthen the resilience of communities; maintain, sustain, and build on routine systems; and leverage broader capacities for emergency preparedness and response. This Call to Action was released at the Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET): Global Meeting for Future Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics held on 24-26 April 2023 in Geneva, Switzerland.
translations.reference: daidaito cikin wannan aiki na da muhimmanci domin shiryawa annobar. akwai buƙatar al'umma bakiɗaya ta ɗau matakin da zai tabbatar da nasarar ain da aka sa a gaba. ya kamata aiwatarwar ta ƙarfafi guiwar al'umma wajen gina tabbataccen tsari na yau da kullum da cin moriyar ƙarin dama ta ɗaura ɗamarar ko-ta-kwana. An yi wannan kira ne a lokacin zaburarwa da ɗaura ɗamarar tunƙarar duk wata barazana (PRET): Taron Tattaunawa kan Makomar Annobar Cutar Numfashi ta Duniya da aka yi a ranar 24 ga Afirilu a Geneva, Switzerland.

translations.source: WHO urges increased implementation of recommended tools to combat malaria Marking World Malaria Day, the World Health Organization (WHO) is issuing a call for increased implementation of new and existing interventions to save lives from malaria. Nearly 1.5 million children at high risk of illness and death from malaria in Ghana, Kenya and Malawi have now received their first dose of the first malaria vaccine, RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S), thanks to an ongoing pilot programme coordinated by WHO. The malaria vaccine pilots, launched in 2019, are increasing equity in access to malaria prevention for the most vulnerable and are saving lives. If implemented broadly, WHO estimates that malaria vaccines could save the lives of tens of thousands of children each year.
translations.reference: Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta yi kira da a ƙara yawan yadda ake amfani da abubuawan da za su taimaka wajen yaƙi da zazzaɓin cizon sauro a yayin Ranar Yaƙi da Zazzaɓin Cizon Sauro ta Duniya, Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) ta yi kira da a ƙara ƙoƙari wajen amfani da sabbi da matakan da ake da su da wajen ceto rayuka daga zazzaɓin cizon sauro. Yara kusan miliyan 1.5 da ke fuskantar barazanar kamuwa ko rasa rai sabili da cutar maleriya daga ƙasashen Ghana, Kenya da Malawi, sun samu kashin farko na allurar rigakafin cutar maleriya RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S). Godiya ga shirin na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya. kashin farko na rigakafin maleriya wanda aka ƙaddamar a 2019 na cigaba da samar da hanyoyin kariya al'umma da kuma ceton rayuka. idan aka faɗaɗa shirin, Hukumar Lafiya ta duniya na ganin za a iya kai wa ga ceto rayukan dubun nan yara a kowacce shekara.

translations.source: “We have the tools to drive down malaria, a package of interventions that includes vector control, preventive medicines, testing, and treatment,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of WHO. “These are joined by a safe and effective malaria vaccine, which could save the lives of tens of thousands children every year. With sustained investment and scaled-up efforts to reach those most at risk, malaria elimination in many countries is in reach. "World Malaria Day 2023 is being marked under the theme “Time to deliver zero malaria: invest, innovate, implement”. Within this theme, WHO is urging more effective implementation of available tools and strategies to prevent, diagnose and treat malaria, particularly among marginalized populations.
translations.reference: a cewar darakta janar na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO), muna da kayan kai ɗauki da za mu iya rage tasirin cutar maleriya da suka haɗa da: abin magance ƙwayoyin yaɗa cutar da magugunan rigakafi da na gwaje-gwaje da na maganin cutar. waɗannan sun ƙunshi ingantattun rigakafin cutar maleriya da za su kai ga ceton dubun nan yara a kowacce shekara. akwai yuwuwar kawo ƙarshen cutar maleriya a ƙasashe da dama, idan aka ƙara himma wajen cigaba da kai ɗauki ga waɗanda suka fi fuskantar baranaza. An yi wa ranar cutar maleriya ta duniya ta 2023 take da lokacin kawo ƙarshen maleriya bakiɗaya: zuba jari, fikira da ɗaukar mataki. Taken, wata hanya ce da Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ke son yin kira wajen amfani da kayan aiki da sahihan hanyoyi domin hana kamuwa da ba da magungunan magance maleriya musamman a tsakanin talakawa.

translations.source: According to the latest World malaria report, published in December 2022, there were an estimated 247 million new cases of malaria in 2021. The WHO African Region continues to shoulder the heaviest burden of the disease – accounting for an estimated 95% of all malaria cases (234 million) and 96% of all deaths (593 000) in 2021. Nearly 80% of malaria deaths in the African Region were among children under the age of five. New strategies and tools Countries have made some progress in expanding access to malaria services for most-at-risk populations.
translations.reference: a cewar wani rahoto na baya-bayan nan na duniya kan maleriya da aka wallafa a watan Disambar 2022, akwai sabbin alƙaluman kamuwa da maleriya da ya kai miliyan 247 a 2021. Reshen Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) da ke Afirka ne ya fi samun yawan masu fama da cutar, inda aka samu alƙaluman kamuwa da cutar maleriya da kaso 95% (miliyan 234) da kaso 96% na waɗanda suka rasa rayukansu (593000) a 2021. Kaso 80% ne waɗanda suka rasa ransu a yankin Afrika saboda cutar maleriya yara ne da ke ƙasa da shekaru biyar a duniya. Sabbin hanyoyi da kayan aiki Wasu ƙasashe sun yi nasarar kai wa ga faɗaɗa kai ɗauki ga al'ummatan da suka fi fuskantar baranar kamuwa da maleriya.

Kusan kashi 80% na mutuwar sakamakon malaria a yankin Afirka na faruwa a cikin yara 'yan kasa da shekara biyar. Sabbin hanyoyin da kayan aiki Kasashe sun samu ci gaba a cikin fadada hanyoyin samun sabisai na malaria ga mutanen da ke cikin hadarin mafi yawa.

**Notes on Translation Choices:**

translations.source: Despite some progress, many people at high risk of malaria still lack access to services that can prevent, detect and treat the disease. Challenges in expanding access to malaria services have been compounded, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa, by the COVID-19 pandemic, converging humanitarian crises, restricted funding, weak surveillance systems, and declines in the effectiveness of core malaria-fighting tools. To address these threats and support countries in building more resilient malaria programmes, WHO recently published new strategies and frameworks, including: a new strategy to contain antimalarial drug resistance in Africa; a new initiative to stop the spread of Anopheles stephensi in urban environments;
translations.reference: Duk da wannan nasara, da yawa daga cikin al'umma na cigaba da fuskantar barazar kamuwa da cutar maleriya saboda rashin samun ɗaukin kariya da gano da magance cutar. Kai tallafi da shafi cutar maleriya ya gamu da tazgaro musamman a yankin Saharar Afirka saboda annobar Kwabid-19 da matsalar jin-ƙai da ƙarancin samun kuɗin ɗaukar nauyi da rashin sa-ido da ƙarancin wadatattun kayan aikin yaƙi da cutar maleriya. A kwanakin baya ne Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta wallafa wasu sabbin dabaru da hanyoyi na magance barazana da kai ɗauki ga ƙasashe domin samar da ingantattun shirye-shirye kan cutar maleriya.: sabbin dabarun da za su ƙunshi magungunan rigakafin maleriya a Afirka; muhalli;
translations.hypothesis:

**Hausa Translation:**

Kodayake an samu wasu ci gaba, mutane da yawan jama'a suna cikin haddasa cutar malaria har yanzu basu da isasshen samun sabobin da za su hana, gano, da magance cutar. Matsalolin da ke hana yawan samun sabobin hana malaria sun karu, musamman a Afirka ta Kudu da Kudu, sakamakon jarrabawar COVID-19, rikice-rikicen bil'adama da ke haɗawa, kudin da aka ƙiƙirƙiri, tsarin bincike mara ƙarfi, da raguwar aikin da aka yi wa kayan aikin yaki da malaria. Don warware wannan barazana da tallafawa ƙasashe wajen gina shirye-shiryen malaria masu jure zafi, WHO a kwanan nan ta buga sabbin dabarun da ƙungiyoyin, gami da: sabon dabarun kawar da jinin maganin malaria a Afirka; sabon shiri don dakatar da yaduwar Anopheles stephensi a cikin biranen.

**Explanation of Choices & Considerations for Hausa:**

*   **"Despite some progress" - "Kodayake an samu wasu ci gaba"**: This is a standard and clear way to express "despite" in Hausa.
*   **"at high risk of malaria" - "suna cikin haddasa cutar malaria"**:  "Cikin haddasa" conveys the idea of being vulnerable or susceptible to the disease.
*   **"lack access to services" - "basu da isasshen samun sabobin"**:  "Basu da isasshen" means "they don't have enough" or "they lack sufficient." "Sabobin" is the plural of "sababi" which means "services" or "means."
*   **"prevent, detect and treat" - "hana, gano, da magance"**: These are direct and commonly used Hausa verbs for these actions.

*   **"converging humanitarian crises" - "rikice-rikicen bil'adama da ke haɗawa"**: "Rikice-rikice" means "crises" and "haɗawa" means "converging" or "coming together."
*   **"restricted funding" - "kudin da aka ƙiƙirƙiri"**: "Ƙiƙirƙiri" means "limited" or "restricted."
*   **"weak surveillance systems" - "tsarin bincike mara ƙarfi"**: "Tsarin bincike" means "surveillance system" and "mara ƙarfi" means "weak."
*   **"declines in the effectiveness" - "raguwar aikin"**: "Raguwa" means "decrease" or "decline" and "aikin" means "effectiveness" or "work."

*   **"To address these threats" - "Don warware wannan barazana"**: "Warware" means "to solve" or "to address" and "barazana" means "threat."
*   **"support countries in building more resilient malaria programmes" - "da tallafawa ƙasashe wajen gina shirye-shiryen malaria masu jure zafi"**: "Tallafawa" means "to support," "gina" means "to build," and "masu jure zafi" means "resilient" (literally "those who endure heat").

*   **"antimalarial drug resistance" - "jinin maganin malaria"**: "Jinin" means "resistance" in this context.
*   **"stop the spread" - "dakatar da yaduwar"**: "Dakatar da" means "to stop" and "yaduwar" means "spread."
*   **"urban environments" - "biren"**: This is the Hausa word for "cities" or "urban areas."

**Important Notes for Low-Resource Languages:**

translations.source: a new framework, developed jointly by WHO and UN-Habitat, to guide city leaders in urban malaria control. a new toolkit to help countries assess their malaria surveillance systems and identify areas for investment WHO has also increased the transparency, flexibility and access to its malaria recommendations. The consolidated WHO Guidelines for malaria are now available through two digital platforms: MAGICapp and the “Malaria Toolkit” app. WHO encourages countries to tailor the recommendations to local disease settings for maximum impact.
translations.reference: wani sabon tsari na haɗin guiwa da aka samar tsakanin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya da Sashen Gina Birane na Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya, zai yi wa shugabanni jagoranci wajen kula da cutar maleriya a birane. a akwai sabbin kayan aikin da za su taimaka wa ƙasashe wajen auna matakin cutar maleriya da gano ɓangarorin da ya kamata a kashe wa kuɗi Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta ƙara faɗaɗa damar kai wa ga shawarwarinta kan cutar maleriya. ana iya zamun ƙunshin shawarwarin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta manhajoji guda biyu: MAGICapp da Malaria Toolkitapp. ƙasashe za su iya magance cututtuka domin ƙara cin moriyar shawarwarin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya.

translations.source: Prospects for new interventions Continued investment in the development and deployment of new malaria vaccines and next-generation tools will be key to achieving the 2030 global malaria targets. A second malaria vaccine, the R21/Matrix-M (R21) vaccine, if approved, could help close the sizable gap between supply and demand and further reduce child illness and death from malaria. It is a priority for WHO to continue the thorough and efficient expert review of the R21 malaria vaccine once additional key safety and efficacy data from the ongoing R21 phase 3 trial are available and provided to WHO. Phased introductions of the RTS,S malaria vaccine in additional countries in Africa are expected to begin in early 2024.
translations.reference: sabbin hanyoyin kai ɗauki cigaba da tallafawa bunƙasawa da rarraba sabon rigakafin maleriya da sabunta fasahar kayayyaki hanya ce da za kai ga cimma muradan kawo ƙarshen maleriya na 2030. Nau'in rigakafin maleriya na biyu, R21/Matrix-M (R21), zai iya taimakawa wajen wadatuwarsa ga masaya tare da rage yawan yara masu fama da zazzaɓi da ransa rai saboda cutar ta maleriya. abu ne mai muhimmanci ga Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya cigaba da binciken kwakwaf kan nau'in rigakafin maleriya na R21 har sai ta samu rahoton ƙarin tabbaci game da ingancin bayanai kan gwajin R21 karo na uku da ake yi da zarar an kammala. ana sa ran kai rigakafin maleriya na RTSS zuwa wasu ƙasashen Afirka a farkon shekarar 2024.

translations.source: In the vector control space, there are 28 new products in the R&D pipeline. Tools under evaluation include, for example, new types of insecticide-treated nets, targeted baits that attract mosquitoes, spatial repellents, lethal house lures (eaves tubes) and genetic engineering of mosquitoes. Researchers are prioritizing the development of non-ACT treatments in the field of antimalarial medicines due to the emergence and spread of partial artemisinin resistance. The development of next-generation medicines, such as "triple ACTs," which use a combination of artemisinin and two partner drugs, is underway to reduce the risk of drug resistance. Notes to the editor:For more information on the WHO World Malaria Day campaign, visit: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023
translations.reference: akwai sabbin kayayyaki a fannin bincike a ɓangaren kula da kwayoyin da ke yaɗa cuta. Ga misali, kayayyakin da ake kan nazarinsu sun haɗa da, sabbin nau'o'in gidan sauro mai ɗauke da sinadarin kashe ƙwari, abubuwan yi wa sauro ƙofar-rago, abin kore sauro da gidan kashe sauro da sauya zubin halittar sauro. Masu bincike a ɓangaren samar da rigakafin yaƙi da cutar maleriya sun fi mai da hankali ga cigaban hanyoyin warkarwa da ba na ACT ba saboda ƙarancin tasirin sinadarin atimisinin. ana sa ran cewa cigaba wajen samar da magugunan da za su zama zakaran gwajin dafi a nan gaba kamar "Triaple ACTs" wanda ya ƙunshi sinadarin atimisinin da wasu magunguna biyu, zai rage fargabar rashin tasirin magani. abin lura ga edita: Domin samun ƙarin bayani game da Ranar Hukumasr Lafiya ta Duniya, ziyarci: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023

translations.source: mRNA Technology Transfer Programme moves to the next phase of its development Inauguration of the mRNA technology Hub facility at Afrigen, South Africa. Partners gather in Cape Town to discuss the next phase of the mRNA vaccine development and knowledge sharing. Over 200 international participants working with the mRNA Technology Transfer Programme met in Cape Town this week for their first face-to-face meeting. Joined by World Health Organization (WHO) Director General, Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus and Dr. Joe Phaahla, Minister of Health and Minister of Trade and Industry, Mr. Ebrahim Patel of South Africa together with high-level officials from funding countries, this unique meeting reviewed the progress since WHO and Medicines Patent Pool (MPP) launched it in June 2021.
translations.reference: Shirin Kamfanin Musayar Fasaha ta mRNA ya shiga mataki na biyu a cigaban da yake samu. za a ƙaddamar da Kamfanin Fasaha na mRNA a Afrigen, a Afrika ta Kudu. abokan haɗin guiwar za su haɗu ne a garin Cape Town domin tattauna mataki na gaba na cigaba da musayar ilimi game da rigakafin mRNA. sama da mutane 200 masu ba da gudumuwa daga faɗin duniya da ke aiki da Kamfanin Musayar Fasaha ta mRNA ne za su haɗu a wannan sati a Cape Town domin ganawa kai tsaye da juna. darakta-janar na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO), Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus da Ministan Lafiya, Dr. Joe Phaahla, da Ministan Kasuwanci da Masana'antu, Mr. Ebrahim Patel na Afirka ta Kudu tare da manyan jami'ai daga ƙasashen da ke ba da tallafi; duk sun haɗu a wurin taron domin sake duba irin cigaban da aka samu tun bayan da Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya da Kanfanin Haɗa Magungunan suka ƙaddamar da ita a 2021.

translations.source: “I am delighted to be here in Cape Town with our partners to support a sustainable model for mRNA technology transfer to give low- and middle-income countries equitable access to vaccines and other lifesaving health products,” said WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “I am immensely proud of the achievement of all those involved in this project; in less than two years we have shown that when we work collaboratively, we succeed collectively.” The meeting participants include biomanufacturing partners from 15 countries in the Programme, leading experts, industry, civil society representatives, and funders. During the five-day meeting, participants will share progress and discuss critical enablers for the sustainability of the Programme such as intellectual property issues and regulatory aspects, as well as the science of mRNA technologies and key applications relevant to LMICs in other disease areas such as HIV and tuberculosis. The COVID-19 pandemic has underscored that gross inequity exists in access to health products, especially vaccines.
translations.reference: Na yi farin cikin kasancewa a nan Cape Town tare da abokan haɗin guiwa na samar da shiri mai ɗorewa na Kamfanin Musayar Fasaha ta mRNA domin tallafawa ƙasashe masu ƙaramin ƙarfi wajen samun rigafi da sauran magunguna inganta lafiya kamar kowa; a cewar darakta-janar na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. Ina alfahari da duk waɗanda suka ba da gudunmuwa a wannan aiki wajen samun nasara cikin abin da bai wuce shekara biyu ba wanda hakan ke tabbatar da cewa idan aka yi aiki bakiɗaya, za mu samu nasara tare. taron ya haɗa da masu haɗin guiwa daga kamfanin kimiyya daga ƙasashe 15 da ke cikin shirin, da ƙwararru da masana'antu da wakilan al'umma da masu zuba hannun jari. Ana sa ran a yayin wannan taro na kwanaki biyar, mahalarta taron za su yi musayar hanyoyin samar da cigaba ɗorawar shirin kamar batu na haƙƙin mallaka da dokokin gudanarwa da fasahar mRNA da muhimman hanyoyin LMICs da suka shafi wasu cututtuka kamar cuta mai karya garkuwar jiki da kuma tarin fuka. Annobar Kwabid-19 ta yi matuƙar fallasa mummunan rashin daidaito na neman lafiya, musamman game da rigakafi.

"Na yi alfahari sosai da kwarewar dukkan wadanda suka shaidawa a wannan aiki; a cikin kusan shekaranni biyu, mun nuna cewa lokacin da muke aiki tare, muna samun nasara tare."  Taron ya haɗa da abokan bugawa daga kasashe 15 a cikin Shirin, masana kimiyya, masana'antu, wakilan jami'an farar kai, da masu ba da kuɗi. A lokacin taron da zai ci gaba da tsawon kwanaki biyar, mahalata za su raba ci gaba da tattauna abubuwan da ke ba da damar dacewa da Shirin, irin su matsalar mallakar babi, da bangaren dokokin, da kuma ilimin fasahar mRNA da kuma amfani da muhimmin aikin da ke da alaƙa da kasashe kamar yadda suke da matsala da kuma matsakaicin kudin shiga a fannin cuta kamar HIV da iska. Cutawar COVID-19 ta nuna cewa babbar rashin daidaito ta kasance a kan samun abubuwan da ke adana rayuka, musamman alluran rigakafi.

**Notes on Translation Choices & Considerations:**

*   **"mRNA technology transfer"**: I translated this as "bugawa ƙwayoyin mRNA" (mRNA vaccine production/manufacturing).  This is a common and understandable way to express this concept in Hausa.
*   **"Low- and middle-income countries"**: Translated as "ƙasashe da ke da matsala da kuma matsakaicin kudin shiga" (countries with low and medium income). This is the standard Hausa phrasing.
*   **"Equitable access"**: Translated as "samun isasshen kwarewar" (having sufficient access).
*   **"Lifesaving health products"**: Translated as "abubuwan da ke adana rayuka" (things that save lives).
*   **"Biomanufacturing partners"**: Translated as "abokan bugawa" (production partners).
*   **"Intellectual property issues"**: Translated as "matsalar mallakar babi" (problem of ownership of parts/intellectual property).
*   **"Regulatory aspects"**: Translated as "bangaren dokokin" (aspects of law/regulations).
*   **"LMICs"**: I expanded this to "ƙasashe kamar yadda suke da matsala da kuma matsakaicin kudin shiga" for clarity in Hausa. Using abbreviations is less common in Hausa.
*   **"COVID-19 pandemic has underscored"**: Translated as "Cutawar COVID-19 ta nuna" (The COVID-19 pandemic showed).
*   **"Gross inequity"**: Translated as "babbar rashin daidaito" (great inequality).

**Important Considerations for Low-Resource Languages:**

*   **Context is Key:**  Hausa has a rich vocabulary, and the best translation often depends on the specific context.

translations.source: As of March 2023, more than three years after WHO declared COVID-19 a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC), 69.7% of the global population had received at least one dose of a COVID-19 vaccine. Notably, this proportion still remains below 30% in low-income countries (LICs) . This Programme aims to contribute to equitable access to mRNA vaccines by increasing the distribution of sustainable manufacturing capacity across LMICs, enhancing regional and inter-regional collaborations, and developing and empowering a local workforce through tailored and inclusive training and expert support. What is unique in the mRNA Technology Transfer model is the multilateral process that allows sharing of technologies to multiple recipients so that through local and regional production those in need can be reached rapidly. Dr Phaahla, Minister of Health South Africa, said: “What we see here today, is a moment in history, a Programme that is aimed at empowering LMICs through a global collaborative network.
translations.reference: Tun bayan ayyana Kwabid-19 a matsayin lamarin da ya shafi lafiya da ke buƙata kulawar gaggawa a duniya a watan Maris ɗin 2023, kawo 69.7 cikin ɗari na al'umma ne a faɗin duniya suka kai ga samun aƙalla ƙunshi ɗaya na rigakafin Kwabid-19. Abin lura shi ne kaso 30% na wancan adadi sun fito ne daga ƙasashe masu ƙaramin ƙarfi (LICs). Manufar wannan shiri shi ne ba da gudunmawa ta baiɗaya wajen samun rigakafin nRNA ta hanyar ninƙa samar da rigakafin a tsakankanin LMICs da ƙarfafa haɗin guiwa a tsakanin yanki da sauran yankuna da bunƙasawa da ƙarfafa wa masu aiki na ƙasa ta hanyar ba su horo da tallafi. Abin burgewa game da Tsarin Musayar Fasaha shi ne tsarin sanya kowa cikin samun damar musayar fasaha ga mutane daban-daban wajen iya samar da rigakafin domin isa ga masu buƙata cikin sauri. A cewar dakta Phaahla, ministan Lafiya na Afirka ta Kudu; abin da muke gani a yau abu ne na tarihi, shiri ne da manufarsa karfafa wa LMICs da ke faɗin Duniya ta hanyar haɗin guiwa.

translations.source: I am thrilled to see the progress made in such a relatively short time and welcome the support from so many different countries - countries like South Africa that have a strong vibrant biomanufacturing capacity and that are willing to work together, learn from and share with each other.” Minister Blade Nzimande of Higher Education and Science, said: “Ours is the vision of the mRNA Technology Transfer Hub beyond just COVID-19. The capabilities we are building are looking to empower us to deal with other future pandemics whose vaccines could use the same mRNA technology platform. It is a vision that has set its eyes on diseases that are prevalent in our environments such as Malaria, Tuberculosis and HIV and AIDS.” This occasion also marks an important milestone for the Programme with the inauguration of the mRNA technology Hub facility at Afrigen in Cape Town in the presence of Dr. Tedros and Dr. Matshidiso Moeti WHO Regional Director for Africa, Charles Gore Executive Director of MPP and the Honorable Ministers who took part in a ribbon cutting ceremony at Afrigen.
translations.reference: Na yi matuƙar farin cikin ganin cigaban da aka samu a ƙankanin lokaci tare da yin maraba da tallafi da aka samu daga ƙasashe da dama kamar Afirka ta Kudu da ke da Kanfanin Kimiyyar Halittu da kuma ƙoƙarinta na ganin ta koya tare da musayar ilimi a kowa. a cewar ministan Ilimi Mai Zurfi da Kimiyya, Blade Nzimande, mafarkinmu game da Shirin Musayar Fasaha ta mRNA shi ne ta zarce batun Kwabid-19 kaɗai. Muna ƙoƙarin samar da yanayi ne da zai ba mu dama a nan gaba mu iya fuskantar kowacce annoba ta hanyar samar da rigakafinta daga fasahar mRNA. Wannan hangen nesa ne da aka yi kan cututtukan da suka zama gama-gari ga al'ummarmu kamar maleriya da tarin fuka da cuta mai karya garkuwar jiki (HIV da AIDS). Haka kuma wannan taro ya fito da muhimmancin shirin da aka ƙaddamar na Fasahar mRNA da aka yi a Afrigen da ke Cape Town a idon Dakta Tedros da Dakta Matshidiso Moeti wanda darakta ne na Yankin Afirka a Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya da Charles Gore, babban darkta na MPP da kuma ministoci waɗanda suka sa hannu wajen yanka ribon na bikin kaddamarwar.

translations.source: Professor Petro Terblanche, Executive Director of Afrigen said: “The entire Afrigen team are thrilled to reach this important milestone with the completion of the mRNA technology platform. This platform is housed within the end-to-end mRNA vaccine development and production facility where the mRNA Hub Covid 19 vaccine candidate AfriVac 2121 is currently in scale-up phase. Over the last 18 months, Afrigen has undergone an incredible transformation with the support of a network of partners and mentors enabled by this Programme. We have grown our capability and capacity to meet the highest quality standards of mRNA vaccine development, serving the objective to build sustainable capacity in LMICs to produce mRNA vaccines”. Afrigen has successfully established a COVID-19 vaccine manufacturing process at a laboratory scale and is currently scaling up that process to a level suitable for manufacturing vaccine batches to be used in Phase I/ II clinical trials to GMP standards.
translations.reference: Babban Darkta na Afrigen, farfesa Petro Terblanche cewa ya yi, ɗaukacin ma'aikatan Afrigen sun yi farin ciki na cimma wannan gagarumin mataki na kammala tsarin shirin Fasahar mRNA. wannan wuri yana ɗauke da kayayyakin samar da rigakafin mRNA na Kwabid-19 samfurin AfriVac 2121 wanda ake matakin dab da kammala haɗa shi. Sama da watanni 18 Afrigen ke samun cigaba ta hanyar tallafi daga masu haɗin guiwa da mashawarta na wannan shiri. Ƙoƙarinmu na ƙaruwa domin kai wa ga ƙolokuwar mataki na inganci na rigakafin mRNA wanda ginshiƙi ga LMICs mai ɗorewa wajen samar da rigakafin mRNA. Afrigen ta samu nasarar samar da ɗakin gwajin rigakafin Kwabid-19 kuma a yanzu tare da samar da ingantattun ƙananan cibiyoyin samar da rigakafin da za a yi amfani da su a mataki na I da na II na gwaji domin kai wa ga ingancin GMP.
